Japanese: ‥‥睡眠薬を飲まされたの‥‥
English: That was... no accident...

Japanese: あれは‥‥事故じゃなかったわ
English: I was drugged... with sleeping pills...

Japanese: あたしは‥‥殺されたのよ ‥‥アイツに‥‥
English: I was murdered... by that person...

Japanese: ‥‥だから‥‥復讐してやった‥‥
English: That's why... I took... my revenge...

Japanese: 当然でしょ？　のどか‥‥
English: It's only fair... Isn't it... ...Ini?

Japanese: ‥‥せっかくまた、 なるほどくんに会えたのに‥‥。
English: I finally get to see you again, and...

Japanese: きみのせいじゃないよ。
English: It's not your fault. You didn't do it.

Japanese: あたし‥‥殺しちゃったんだ。 ‥‥あの人を。
English: No, I... I did it... I killed that person.

Japanese: あれは、きみじゃなかったんだ。
English: But that wasn't you.

Japanese: ううん。 おんなじことだよ‥‥。
English: It doesn't matter. It might as well have been me.

Japanese: （まさか、こんなことに なるなんて‥‥）
English: (I can't believe something like this happened...)

Japanese: （‥‥すべての始まりが、 アタマの中によみがえった）
English: (The events of that gloomy, rainy afternoon that started this whole mess...)

Japanese: （‥‥あの、うっとうしい 雨の午後の情景が‥‥）
English: (keep playing through my mind...)

Japanese: ６月１６日　午後　３時３４分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: June 16, 3:34 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥うっとうしい雨です。
English: What depressing rain...

Japanese: わかりますか？ うっとうしいんですよ、私は！
English: Do you understand how depressed that makes me!? Do you!?

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Uh, I guess...

Japanese: それ以上にハラが立つのが、 天気予報のお姉さんです。
English: But actually, more than being depressed, I'm angry. Angry at that weather girl on TV.

Japanese: 《梅雨の合間、つかのまの お日さまがのぞくでしょう》‥‥
English: "I think our little rainy spell will take a break with a day of sun!"

Japanese: お姉さんはこう、ハッキリ言った！ 断言してもいい！
English: ...is what that girl said! I'd stake my life on it!

Japanese: それはそれは‥‥。
English: Well, it's the weather...

Japanese: だから私は今日、 カサを持ってこなかった。
English: But that's why I didn't bother to bring an umbrella today.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、このザマです！ １５万円のスーツが台無しだ！
English: What nerve, calling herself a weather girl! She's going to hear about my $1500 suit!

Japanese: あの‥‥。 それで、ご用件は？
English: Um... About your case...

Japanese: その天気予報のお姉さんを 訴えるんですか？
English: Unless you're planning on suing the weather girl...?

Japanese: これは、シツレイ。
English: I'm sorry.

Japanese: 本題に入る前の、軽い 世間話のツモリだったのですが。
English: I thought that before we got down to business, we could have some friendly chatter.

Japanese: 私、外科医をやっております。 霧崎　哲郎（きりさきてつろう）。
English: My name is Dr. Turner Grey. I'm a surgeon.

Japanese: （この先生に手術されるのは カンベンしてほしいな‥‥）
English: (Remind me never to end up under his knife...)

Japanese: 今日は、この件で やってまいりました。
English: I'm here today because of this incident.

Japanese: 《霧崎外科医院で医療ミス。 １４人の入院患者が死亡》
English: "Malpractice at Grey Surgical Clinic. Fourteen In-Patients Lose Their Lives!"

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 あなたが、あの‥‥？
English: ...Oh. You're... the doctor at...?

Japanese: まったく、ハラ立たしい話です。
English: It's really quite upsetting.

Japanese: いいですか？　これは保証します。 私は、ハラを立てているんです！
English: Did you hear me!? UPSETTING!

Japanese: はいはいはい。 そうなんでしょうね、霧崎先生。
English: Yes, yes, I heard you. I agree, it's quite upsetting, Dr. Grey.

Japanese: ミスをしたのは、看護師なんだ！
English: The one that screwed up was that nurse!

Japanese: １４人を殺したのは、 看護師の投薬ミスだったのですぞ！
English: It was her that got the medications mixed up and killed those 14 patients!

Japanese: しかも！ いいですか、しかもですぞ！
English: And yet! Now listen good! And yet!

Japanese: この看護師は、自分の罪を認める 前に、死んじまったんです！
English: That nurse had the nerve to go and die before admitting to her wrongdoing!

Japanese: あろうことか、この私に なんのことわりもなく！
English: She didn't even offer any sort of explanation to me! And this is the kicker!

Japanese: 勝手に交通事故を起こして、 勝手に車ごとぺちゃんこです！
English: She up and has herself a grand ol' accident and crashes her car!

Japanese: ぺちゃんこですぞ！ わかりますか？　それも、車ごと！
English: Smash! Crunch! Instantly transforming her car into the accordion model!

Japanese: （‥‥そう。今から１年前、 ワイドショーは大さわぎだった）
English: (That's right... About a year ago, all the tabloid shows were talking about it.)

Japanese: （１４人も亡くなった医療ミスと、 看護師のナゾの事故死‥‥）
English: (Fourteen victims of medical malpractice and the nurse's mysterious fatal accident.)

Japanese: （事故は霧崎先生が仕組んだ、 なんてウワサもあった）
English: (There were rumors that Dr. Grey had actually caused that crash.)

Japanese: （医療ミスの真相をかくすため、 永久にダマらせたのだ、と‥‥）
English: (People said it was to forever hide the person responsible for the malpractice incident.)

Japanese: なんで私が‥‥ いいですか、もう一度言いますよ！
English: Why would I...? Now, I'm just going to say this once --

Japanese: なんでこの私が、あの看護師を 殺さなければならないんですか？
English: Why on Earth would I want to kill that nurse?

Japanese: １４人殺したのは、彼女なんだ！ 私じゃない！
English: She's the one who killed those 14 patients! Not me!

Japanese: おキノドクだと思いますけどね。
English: I think you need to take a deep breath, doctor, and focus...

Japanese: でも、もう１年も前の話ですよ。 なんで、今さら‥‥？
English: More to the point, this case is over a year old. Why bring it up now...?

Japanese: 今だからこそ！ なんとかせねばならんのです！
English: That's simple! Because the situation is slipping out of my grasp.

Japanese: いいですか！　私の病院には、 患者が寄りつかない。
English: This just won't do. My clinic isn't seeing nearly as many patients nowadays.

Japanese: どういうことかわかりますか？ 来ないんですよ、患者が！
English: Do you understand what that means!? It means they're not coming to my clinic!

Japanese: （‥‥そりゃそうか。 ぼくだって行きたくないもん）
English: (You don't say...? If it were me, I don't think I'd want to go either.)

Japanese: それで‥‥ぼくに、どうしろと？
English: So... what would you like me to do about it...?

Japanese: 私の無実を証明する お手伝いをしていただきたい。
English: I want you to help me prove I'm innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ぼくは弁護士で、 探偵じゃありませんよ。
English: Um, you do realize I'm a lawyer, not a private investigator, right?

Japanese: いや。これは、あなたにしか おねがいできないのです。
English: No, this is something only you can do.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵。 ‥‥もちろん、ごぞんじですね？
English: Maya Fey. You know this girl, correct?

Japanese: （アヤサトマヨイ‥‥ どうして、その名前が‥‥！）
English: (Maya! Why would he know her name...!?)

Japanese: あなた、彼女の下で しばらく働いていたそうですね。
English: I heard you were working under her for a little while.

Japanese: まあ、たしかに‥‥。って、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: Well, yes... I mean... Hey, wait a second here!

Japanese: ぼくが“彼女の下で” 働いていたんですか？
English: I worked UNDER her!?

Japanese: ええ。ちがうんですか？ 彼女はそう言っていましたが‥‥。
English: Yes. Am I wrong? She told me, so I'm not quite sure if...

Japanese: ‥‥以前、いっしょに 法廷に立っていたんです。
English: Well, we worked a few trials together.

Japanese: ‥‥今はもう、“修行”に もどってしまいましたけど。
English: But she went back to her hometown to undergo more training.

Japanese: ええ、聞いてますよ。 おさびしいでしょう。
English: Ah, yes, I heard. It must be lonely for you.

Japanese: な、そんな！ べ、べつにぼくは‥‥
English: Wh-What!? Wait... I'm not...!

Japanese: 彼女の“修行”、リッパに 実をむすんだようですよ。
English: She must be an extraordinary girl to handle such rigid training.

Japanese: えっ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 私の知り合いに、オカルトに くわしい人がいまして。
English: I have an acquaintance who knows a lot about these mystical sorts of things.

Japanese: 紹介してくれたのです。 綾里　真宵さんをね。
English: She's the one who introduced me to Ms. Fey.

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥ そんな有名人だったのか？）
English: (Is Maya... really that famous...?)

Japanese: で、私。 このたび、依頼したんですよ。
English: And I've already set up an appointment.

Japanese: ナニをですか？
English: For what?

Japanese: 霊媒ですよ。 決まってるでしょう！
English: What else!? A channeling!

Japanese: 勝手に死んだ、あの看護師を 呼びだしてもらうんです！
English: I'm going to have her call the spirit of that nurse that went and got herself killed!

Japanese: な、なるほど‥‥。
English: O-Oh.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、か。 ‥‥どうしてるかな）
English: (Maya... I wonder what she's up to right now...)

Japanese: あたしたち綾里家の女は代々、 霊媒師として生きてきたの。
English: We, the women of the Fey family, have always been spirit mediums.

Japanese: 綾里の血は、強い霊力を 宿しているから‥‥。
English: It's because the power to communicate with spirits flows strongly through us.

Japanese: （‥‥そんなこと言ってたっけ）
English: (That's what she told me...)

Japanese: （どうやら、一人前に なれたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I guess she's learned to accept her responsibilities and become an adult...)

Japanese: 彼女、修行以外で、だれかの霊を 呼び出すのは初めてなので、
English: Not counting training, this will be her first time channeling a spirit,

Japanese: １つ、条件を出してきましてね。 それで引き受けてくれることに。
English: so she gave me one condition before accepting my request.

Japanese: ‥‥条件？
English: A condition...?

Japanese: それが、あなたなんですよ、 成歩堂さん。
English: The condition is you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたが立ち会うなら、 霊媒を引き受ける、と言うんです。
English: She said that she wouldn't do the channeling without first seeing you again.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくが‥‥
ということで、 つきあっていただけますね！
English: And that is why I've searched you out!

Japanese: あなたも、会いたいでしょう？ ‥‥彼女に。
English: You want to see her too, don't you!?

Japanese: （‥‥というわけで‥‥）
English: (And so...)

Japanese: （ぼくは、彼女のふるさとへ 同行することになった‥‥）
English: (that is how I ended up visiting Maya's hometown with the doctor...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: June 20 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥ずいぶんひさしぶりに 帰ってきた気がする。
English: Phew. It feels like I've been gone forever.

Japanese: でも、たった１日しか たっていないんだよな‥‥
English: But it's only been one day...

Japanese: （あ、そういえば‥‥）
English: (Oh, that's right!)

Japanese: 霧崎先生、あの日、 新聞を持ってきてたっけ‥‥。
English: Where's that newspaper Dr. Grey brought the other day...?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、あった。 デスクの上だ。
English: ...Ah, here it is. Right on top of my desk.

Japanese: （霧崎先生の残した たった１つの手がかりだ‥‥）
English: (This is the only thing he left behind that's going to be of any help...)

Japanese: 証拠品《新聞記事・１》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Newspaper Clipping 1 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: 今は亡くなってしまった所長、 千尋さんの形見‥‥だ。
English: It's a memento of my boss, Mia... who passed away.

Japanese: 散らかりほうだいの事務所だけど、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: The rest of the room may be in shambles, but I always manage to care for this little fella.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、うっすらと ホコリをかぶってるな。
English: ...Actually, I've neglected them for so long, a layer of dust has started to form.

Japanese: たまには‥‥せめて 読むフリだけでもしてみるか。
English: One of these days, I should try to build up the courage to read one... Maybe.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: “ホテル・バンドー” ゴウカな高級ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: ちょっと前までは、ただの ビジネスホテルだったのに。
English: Until recently, it had been a normal, business-class hotel.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: ‥‥新聞記事の切り抜きが あるな‥‥。
English: ...That newspaper clipping is still there.

Japanese: きのうの夜、霧崎先生が 忘れていったものだ。
English: Dr. Grey must have forgotten to take it with him last night.

Japanese: 証拠品《新聞記事・１》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Newspaper Clipping 1 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: I keep it neat for those rare occasions when I actually have a client in the office.

Japanese: テレビのヒーロー “トノサマン”のポスター。
English: A poster of the TV action hero, the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんがここを 去る前に張っていったものだ。
English: Maya stuck it up here right before she left.

Japanese: 弁護士事務所に“トノサマン”は どうかと思うんだけど、
English: I wouldn't say that the Steel Samurai and an attorney's office are a good match, but

Japanese: 『はがしたら呪われる』という コトバがコワくて、はがせない。
English: Maya threatened to curse me with some magic spell... so I guess it can stay.

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時３４分 留置所　面会室
English: June 20, 10:34 AM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: なっ‥‥、 なるほどくん！　なるほどくん！ N...
English: Nick! Nick!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!

Japanese: どうしよう！
English: What am I going to do!?

Japanese: こんなことになるなんて‥‥ 思わなかった！
English: I never imagined it would turn out like this!

Japanese: とにかく、落ちついて！
English: Calm down, Maya! Take deep breaths.

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば‥‥ この子に初めて出会ったときも）
English: (Come to think of it, the first time I met her...)

Japanese: （すべてはこの留置所から 始まったんだっけ‥‥）
English: (It all started right here, in this detention center...)

Japanese: せっかく‥‥
English: I finally...

Japanese: ‥‥せっかくまた、 なるほどくんに会えたのに‥‥。
English: I finally get to see you again, and...

Japanese: きみのせいじゃないよ。
English: It's not your fault. You didn't do it.

Japanese: あたし‥‥殺しちゃったんだ。 ‥‥あの人を。
English: No, I... I did it... I killed that person.

Japanese: あれは、きみじゃなかったんだ。
English: But that wasn't you.

Japanese: ううん。 おんなじことだよ。
English: It doesn't matter. It might as well have been me.

Japanese: あたしの霊力が‥‥ ミジュクだったから。
English: I was too weak... and I... I couldn't control the spirit's power, so...

Japanese: （今はまだ、事件のことに ふれたくない。でも‥‥）
English: (I don't want to bring up the case quite yet, but...)

Japanese: ‥‥わかってるよ。 エンリョなんてしなくていいから。
English: ...You don't have to be nice to me. I understand.

Japanese: （‥‥集められる情報は まあ、こんなところかな‥‥）
English: (...I guess this is about all the information I'm going to gather for today...)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、また来るから。 用意しておいてくれよ。
English: I'll be back later, Maya. In the meantime, please make sure you prepare it, OK?

Japanese: え。‥‥何を？
English: Huh? "It"? What is "it"?

Japanese: 決まってるだろ。 依頼状だよ。弁護の。
English: The document requesting me to be your attorney, of course.

Japanese: でも‥‥。
English: But...

Japanese: どうした？
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: いいの？　あたし、犯人なんだよ。 サツジンハンなんだよ！
English: Are you sure? I mean, I'm guilty! I'm a murderer!

Japanese: まだ、そう決まった ワケじゃない。
English: No one's decided that yet.

Japanese: あたしが殺しちゃったんだ！ こ、この手で‥‥あの人を‥‥！
English: But I did! I killed that person... with these... two hands...!

Japanese: もう、いいから。
English: That's enough, Maya.

Japanese: 弁護したってムダだよ！ どうせ、負けちゃう‥‥
English: It's hopeless! If you defend me, you'll lose, I'm sure...

Japanese: やめるんだ！
English: Stop it!
...Help me...

Japanese: なるほどくん、助けて‥‥ こわい‥‥。
English: Nick, help me... I'm scared...

Japanese: なんとかする。 審理はいつ？
English: Don't worry, I will. When is the trial?

Japanese: あした、みたい。
English: It sounds like tomorrow.

Japanese: （‥‥いつもどおり、 時間がないな‥‥）
English: (As usual, zero prep time...)

Japanese: じゃ、行くよ。
English: Alright. I'm going now.

Japanese: 待って。
English: Wait.

Japanese: これ‥‥。
English: This jewel...

Japanese: 勾玉（まがたま）っていうの。 あたしの、お守り。
English: This is called a Magatama. It's a magical charm and it's always protected me.

Japanese: これを、はみちゃんに‥‥。 きっと、力を貸してくれるから。
English: Give this to Pearly... And I'm sure she'll lend you her spiritual powers.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんから 《勾玉》をもらった。
English: Magatama received from Maya.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: タイクツなのだろうか。 こっちには見向きもしない。
English: I wonder if he's bored. Not that I can tell since he never looks over this way.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんて、家元なんだって？ “倉院流霊媒道”の。
English: So you're the Master or something of the Kurain School of Channeling...?

Japanese: 今の家元は、おかあさんだけど。
English: Actually, my mother is for now.

Japanese: たぶん、将来はあたしが。 ‥‥親子で受けつぐものだから。
English: But since that title is passed from mother to daughter, I guess I will be someday...

Japanese: 霊媒って、みんなウソだと 思っていたんだけどさ。
English: But many people think that channeling isn't real, that it's all just an act.

Japanese: あたしたちのことは 信じてくれるの？
English: You believe in us, don't you?

Japanese: うん。‥‥目の前で見ているから。
English: Yeah... because I've seen it with my own eyes.

Japanese: （そう。真宵ちゃんに霊が 乗り移ると‥‥）
English: (When Maya's channeling a spirit...)

Japanese: （その声ばかりか、すがたまで 変わってしまうんだ）
English: (not only her voice, but her whole physical appearance changes.)

Japanese: （まさに、 超常現象ってヤツだな‥‥）
English: (It's a supernatural phenomenon that occurs every time.)

Japanese: （今回も‥‥そうだった）
English: (It happened this time, too...)

Japanese: （霧崎先生は『看護師を呼び出す』 って言ってたから‥‥）
English: (Dr. Grey said that he wanted to call the nurse, so...)

Japanese: （きっと、あのすがたが そうなんだろうな‥‥）
English: (Maya's body must have taken on that nurse's appearance...)

Japanese: いったい、《対面の間》で 何があったんだ？
English: What exactly happened in the Channeling Chamber?

Japanese: 先生とあの部屋に入って、 カギをかけて‥‥
English: I went into that room with Dr. Grey, locked the door...

Japanese: 向かい合って座ったの。
English: and sat down across from him.

Japanese: うん。
English: OK...

Japanese: ２人で目をつぶって‥‥ あたしは、呼びかけた。
English: We closed our eyes... and I began the channeling.

Japanese: （何やら、 神秘的な世界だよな‥‥）
English: (This is sounding kind of creepy... like the beginning of a scary story...)

Japanese: ‥‥あたしにわかるのは、 ここまで。
English: And that's the last thing I remember...

Japanese: え。
English: What?

Japanese: 霊が宿ると、あたしの意識は なくなっちゃうから。
English: After a spirit comes into my body, I lose my self, my consciousness.

Japanese: じゃあ、おぼえてないの？ ‥‥ぜんぜん？
English: Then, you don't remember a single thing?

Japanese: ‥‥気がついたら、 おばさまに抱かれていたの。
English: When I came to, I was being held in my aunt's arms.

Japanese: 服に‥‥血がついていて‥‥。
English: And there was blood... all over my clothes.

Japanese: あ。でも‥‥。 夢を、見たような気がする。
English: Oh! But... I do remember having a dream.
A dream...?

Japanese: どんな夢を見たの？ ‥‥霊媒中に。
English: What kind of dream did you have while you were channeling...?

Japanese: うーん‥‥ハッキリ おぼえてないんだけど‥‥
English: Um... I can't remember it all that well, but...

Japanese: あたしは死んじゃっていて、 土の中に埋められちゃうの。
English: I was dead and buried in the ground.

Japanese: 身動きができなくて、 苦しくなって‥‥
English: I couldn't move... and it was unbearable... Really suffocating...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな夢。
English: ...It went something like that.

Japanese: ああ‥‥そうなんだ。 （コメントしづらいユメだな）
English: I... I see... (I have no idea what to say to that...)

Japanese: ‥‥なつかしいニオイがした。
English: There was a really familiar smell too...

Japanese: 土の中で？
English: Under the ground?

Japanese: うん。なんだか、 なつかしいニオイ‥‥。
English: Yeah, I can't quite place it, but I know I've smelled that same scent before...

Japanese: それ、はみちゃんに 見せてみて。
English: Make sure you show that to Pearly, OK, Nick?

Japanese: きっと、役に立つから。
English: I'm sure she'll be a big help to you.

Japanese: ゴメンね。 ‥‥特に思いつくこと、ないや。
English: Sorry, Nick, but there's nothing really special about it.

Japanese: 取り調べのとき‥‥ ツラかったんだよ。
English: I felt terrible during the questioning session...

Japanese: だって‥‥イトノコさん、 あたしのこと‥‥
English: I mean, Gumshoe kept looking at me...

Japanese: とっても悲しそうな目で 見るんだもん‥‥。
English: with these really sad eyes...

Japanese: ！！ ‥‥あ、あたしが‥‥こ、ころし
English: T-The... That's the guy I k-killed...

Japanese: あ‥‥ご、ごめん！ （この写真は見せちゃダメだろ！）
English: Ack! I'm sorry! (Why did I pull this picture out!?)

Japanese: ‥‥見たくない。 あたしのカオなんて‥‥。
English: I... I can't face myself...

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (I should've known better...)

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥だよね。
English: That's Lotta Hart... right?

Japanese: 彼女、またよけいな写真 撮ってくれちゃってさ。
English: That girl is still after the next sensational photo.

Japanese: ふふ‥‥。 あいかわらずだね。
English: Ha ha. I guess some things never change.

Japanese: おばさま‥‥。あたし、 カオ見せられないよ。
English: I... I can't face my aunt right now...

Japanese: こ、こんな事件、 起こしちゃって‥‥。
English: Not now... that this has happened...

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ。 彼女は、きみの味方だから。
English: It's alright, Maya. I'm sure she understands. She's on our side, after all.

Japanese: うん‥‥。 それはわかってるけど‥‥。
English: Yeah, I know... But still...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、あたしなんかより、 はみちゃんが‥‥
English: Pearly... She definitely has more than me...

Japanese: ？　‥‥なんのこと？
English: Huh? What are you talking about?

Japanese: 家元。‥‥きっと、 あたしより霊力も強いし、
English: Who I think the next Master should be... I mean, she's got more spiritual power than me,

Japanese: 修行だって キチンとやってるし‥‥。
English: and she really works hard at her training...

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。かなり 落ちこんでるな‥‥）
English: (Talk about bringing the mood down even further... *sigh*)

Japanese: あの‥‥このヒトのこと なんだけど‥‥
English: So... what do you think about this person...?

Japanese: （今、このヒトのことを 聞いても、しかたないな）
English: (I guess I can't expect her to know anything about this person.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: June 20 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは‥‥ 取り調べ中らしい）
English: (Maya must still be in questioning...)

Japanese: （刑事さんたち‥‥あまりツラく あたらないでくれるといいけど）
English: (I hope the detectives are taking it easy on her, but...)

Japanese: （しかたないな‥‥。 また、あとで来てみよう）
English: (these kinds of cases aren't taken lightly. I guess I'll come back later.)

Japanese: ６月１９日　午後　１時２５分 倉院の里
English: June 19, 1:25 PM Kurain Village

Japanese: （倉院（くらいん）の里。 真宵ちゃんの‥‥ふるさとか）
English: (So this is Kurain Village; Maya's hometown.)

Japanese: ‥‥や、やあ。こんにちは。
English: W-Why, hello there.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。 ブアイソウな子だな‥‥。
English: Hmm... What an odd little girl.

Japanese: おーい、はみちゃーん！ 待ってよー。
English: Hey! Wait up, Pearly!!

Japanese: （ん？　また だれか来るぞ‥‥）
English: (Hm? Someone else is coming this way...)

Japanese: ああああっ！　なるほどくん！
English: Niiiiiiiick!

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん。 ‥‥ひさしぶり。
English: M-Maya. How are you...? It's good to see you again.

Japanese: うわあ。ホントに来てくれるとは 思わなかったよ！
English: Wow, I didn't think you'd really show up!

Japanese: 大ゲサだなあ。うちの事務所から 電車で２時間じゃないか。
English: It's not that big a deal. You made it sound like you were soooo far away.

Japanese: まあね。
English: Well, maybe I exaggerated... just a little.

Japanese: こんな近いんなら、たまには 遊びにくればよかったのに。
English: It was only 2 hours by train. If I had known you were this close, I'd have visited more.

Japanese: ダメだよ。 あたし、決めたんだもん。
English: No! You're not allowed. I already decided, you know.

Japanese: 一人前になるまで、ひとりで がんばる、って‥‥
English: Until I become an adult, I have to work hard and be strong by myself...

Japanese: まあ、いいや。
English: Oh... well...

Japanese: とにかく、おめでとう。 修行がうまくいって。
English: Anyway, congratulations. I'm glad to hear training is going well for you.

Japanese: へへ‥‥。
English: Heh, heh...

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、あたし そろそろしたくするから。
English: Well, Nick, I've got to get going. It's about time for the channeling to start.

Japanese: え？　あ、ああ。
English: Huh? Oh, yeah...

Japanese: あたしのウチに行ってて。 あそこでやるから。
English: I'm doing the channeling in my house, so come on in, OK?

Japanese: 終わったら、 おいしいみそラーメン作るからね。
English: After I'm done, we can grill up some juicy burgers!

Japanese: わかった。 ‥‥じゃ、がんばって。
English: Alright. Good luck, Maya.

Japanese: うん！
English: Thanks!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥。 元気そうでなによりだな）
English: (Well... At least she's still the same perky Maya.)

Japanese: 巨大な岩が 天をニラみつけている。
English: A giant rock that seems to be reaching for the sky.

Japanese: 古ぼけた立て札に、 由来なんかが書いてあるな。
English: Its origins are written on a beat-up, old sign.

Japanese: “倉院の岩座（いわくら）” ‥‥と呼ばれている巨石らしい。
English: It looks like this giant rock is called the "Kurain Boulder".

Japanese: ムズカシイことが つらつら書かれているが‥‥、
English: There's a really complex explanation written here, but long story short,

Japanese: はやい話が、霊魂の宿る岩、 ということらしい。‥‥たぶん。
English: it's a big stone for spirits to live in... Or something like that...

Japanese: 古びたバス停だ。 “倉院の里”と書かれている。
English: An old bus stop. It says "Kurain Village" on the sign post.

Japanese: バスが‥‥朝、昼、晩の３本か。 やや不便な気がする。
English: The buses seem incredibly inconvenient since they only come 3 times a day.

Japanese: この風景にはなじまないが、 古いタイプの電話ボックスだ。
English: It's an old-style phone booth. I can't say it fits in with this area too well.

Japanese: 携帯電話の電波もとどかないし、 おせわになるかもしれないな。
English: My cell phone doesn't get reception up here, so who knows. I may need it later.

Japanese: ここから見るかぎり、一番 リッパで大きなお屋敷だ。
English: The biggest, most elegant of the buildings I can see from here.

Japanese: これが綾里家、真宵ちゃんの 住んでいる屋敷らしい。
English: This is Fey Manor; the place Maya calls home.

Japanese: 思わず圧倒されそうになるな‥‥。
English: It's easy to be overwhelmed by its presence...

Japanese: ワラぶきの、かわいらしい 民家が並んでいる。
English: Rustic, thatch-roofed houses where the other villagers live line the street.

Japanese: 手前にある、お屋敷のような ハクリョクはない。
English: They're nowhere near as large or as nice as the manor in front of me.

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても、人のすがたが 見あたらないな‥‥。
English: It's kind of odd that I don't see any of the other villagers walking around, though...

Japanese: ついに、霊媒師として 仕事をするんだね。
English: Looks like you've finally become a full-fledged medium, taking on your first job.

Japanese: そうだねー。こんなに早いとは 思わなかったな。
English: Yeah. I didn't think it would be this soon.

Japanese: でも、ちょっとメンドウな話だな。 ‥‥ちゃんとわかってる？
English: But this case... Do you know the messy story behind it...?

Japanese: ふっふっふっ。真宵ちゃんの 情報網をナメちゃダメだよ。
English: Ha ha ha, behold the power of the Maya Intelligence Network!

Japanese: アレでしょ。霧崎先生、医療ミスで １４人殺しちゃったんでしょ。
English: Let's see. Fourteen people died because of malpractice on Dr. Grey's part, right?

Japanese: で、看護師さんにその罪を ゼーンブかぶせるために、
English: And to pin the blame on the nurse,

Japanese: 交通事故に見せかけて 殺しちゃったんだよね！
English: he killed her in what appeared to be a car accident!
Huh?

Japanese: あのさ。 “真宵ちゃんの情報網”って‥‥？
English: Um, so what is this "Maya Intelligence Network" anyway...?

Japanese: ワイドショー。
English: The tabloids.

Japanese: ‥‥だと思った。
English: That's what I thought.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥あはは。 ジョウダンだよ！
English: Ha ha ha. I'm just pulling your leg, Nick!

Japanese: ホントは逆なんでしょ。 事情は先生から聞いてるって！
English: What really happened is the exact opposite. I heard all about it from Dr. Grey.

Japanese: アッタリマエでしょ！
English: Oh, come on, Nick!

Japanese: サツジンハンの依頼なんて、 引き受けないよ！
English: I wouldn't take a crazed killer as a client!

Japanese: コワいもん。
English: That's too scary, even for me.

Japanese: （ま、そりゃそうだな）
English: (Y-Yeah, I guess so.)

Japanese: ここ、真宵ちゃんの ふるさとなんだ。
English: So this is your hometown, huh, Maya?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Yup!

Japanese: 倉院の里。‥‥霊媒師の谷 とも呼ばれてるみたい。
English: Kurain Village... But I've heard people call this place "Medium Valley", too.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。
English: Interesting...

Japanese: 住んでる人、 霊媒師ばっかりなの？
English: So everyone that lives here is a spirit medium?

Japanese: そうだねえ。
English: Pretty much.

Japanese: ウチの親せきは、 たいていそうかな‥‥。
English: Most of my ancestors were too, I think...

Japanese: （綾里家の血は、霊力が 強いらしいからな‥‥）
English: (That's probably true since the Fey ability to communicate with the dead is so strong...)

Japanese: ま。でも、霊媒師は 女の人だけだけど。
English: Actually, only the women of this village are mediums.

Japanese: 男の人は、何をしてるの？
English: So what do the men do?

Japanese: ほとんど、 出かせぎに行ってるよ。
English: They usually work someplace outside the village.

Japanese: そういえば、さっきの女の子‥‥ 知り合い？
English: Oh yeah, I wanted to ask you about that girl I saw earlier. Do you know her?

Japanese: ああ。はみちゃん。
English: Oh, you mean Pearly?

Japanese: はみちゃん？
English: Pearly?

Japanese: 綾里　春美（あやさとはるみ）。 あたしのイトコなんだよ。
English: Pearl Fey. She's my cousin.

Japanese: もお、カワイイでしょ？ あたしに似てさ。
English: Isn't she adorable? Just like yours truly!

Japanese: イトコ‥‥ってことは、 彼女も？
English: Cousin... Wait, then that means... Is she also a...?

Japanese: うん。霊媒師。 はみちゃんはね、テンサイなんだ！
English: Yup. She's a medium, too. Pearly's a real genius when it comes to channeling!

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Hmm, I see.

Japanese: さっき声をかけたら、 圧倒的にムシされたぞ。
English: I hope I didn't scare her when I tried to talk to her earlier.

Japanese: ああ。おばさまにキビしく 教えられているから。
English: Nah, it's not you. My aunt drilled it into her head that

Japanese: “アヤシイ人に声をかけられたら 逃げなさい”って。
English: "if a suspicious-looking person tries to talk to you, run away".

Japanese: あ、アヤシイ人‥‥。
English: S-Suspicious-looking...!?

Japanese: だってなるほどくん、 修行者の装束を着てないでしょ。
English: Well, you're not wearing training clothes, for starters...

Japanese: あたりまえだ。
English: Of course not.

Japanese: はみちゃんはね、 おばさまの宝物だから。
English: Pearly is my aunt's most valued treasure, so she's not allowed to go outside of town.

Japanese: あんまり、そとの世界のこと 知らないんだよ。
English: She doesn't know much about the world outside of Kurain Village.

Japanese: ‥‥おばさま？
English: Aunt?

Japanese: はみちゃんのおかあさん。 あたしの、おばさま。
English: Pearly's mother is my aunt, duh. Come on, Nick.

Japanese: うわ、なつかしいねー。 こきたないバッジ。
English: Ah, it's good to see this tarnished badge again.

Japanese: “こきたない”って‥‥
English: What do you mean by "tarnished"...?

Japanese: でもさ。こういうバッジって、 どうしてみんな、こうなんだろ。
English: Well, look. This badge is like every other.

Japanese: 時間がたつと、どんどん くすんじゃうんだよ、色が。
English: It's bound to lose its color and shine after years and years.

Japanese: いつまでもピカピカなら いいのにね。
English: But you know, polishing it up once in a while wouldn't be a bad idea.

Japanese: さあねえ。 なんにも思い浮かばないな。
English: Hmm, nothing really comes to mind, Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥うれしそうなカオして 言うなよ。
English: Don't say that with such a big smile on your face.

Japanese: ふふ。なんか今日はね。 気分がいいんだ。
English: Ha ha. What can I say? I'm in a good mood today.

Japanese: お。あたしの依頼人だね。
English: Hey, it's my client!

Japanese: “依頼人”‥‥とはまた、 ずいぶんイカめしいね。
English: "Client," huh? You're really serious about your work, aren't you?

Japanese: だって。一度、 言ってみたかったんだよねー。
English: I thought I'd say it once, just to try it out,

Japanese: なるほどくんみたくさ。 “あたしの依頼人”‥‥って。
English: like how you always say, "My client."

Japanese: ふっふっふ。１年前の 真宵ちゃんとはチョットちがうよ。
English: Ha ha ha. I'm warning you! I'm not the same weak Maya you knew a year ago, Nick!

Japanese: お。自信ありそうだね。
English: You seem to have a lot of faith in yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥ま。見てなって。
English: You'll see soon enough.

Japanese: ビックリするから。 ‥‥あたしのラーメン。
English: I think you'll be totally shocked by how good my hamburgers are now!

Japanese: （霊媒のウデのほうは 上がったのか‥‥？）
English: (Were you training to be a medium or a cook?)

Japanese: この人のことだけど‥‥
English: What do you think about this person?

Japanese: あ、知ってた？　なるほどくん。
English: Hey, have I told you this one yet, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥この里にはね。 こんな言い伝えがあるんだよ。
English: We have a legend in this village. It goes like this.

Japanese: “綾里家の女は、みんな美人” ‥‥っていうの。
English: "All the women of the Fey clan are beauties."

Japanese: どう？
English: Well?

Japanese: ‥‥それって“言い伝え”って 言うのか？
English: ...And that's a legend how?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 19 Kurain Village

Japanese: （‥‥ここからじゃ、携帯電話の 電波は届かない）
English: (My cell phone doesn't get reception way out here...)

Japanese: （ぼくたちは、そとの公衆電話から 警察に事件を通報した‥‥）
English: (So I ended up using the phone booth to call the police...)

Japanese: どうや‥‥？
English: And...?

Japanese: すぐこちらへ向かうそうです。
English: They're on their way.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ホンモノや！ ホンモノの怪奇現象やで‥‥。
English: That's good. Lawdy, I saw a genuine "mysterious phenomenon"...

Japanese: （ナツミさん、すっかり ビビってるな‥‥）
English: (She seems really on edge because of all this...)

Japanese: （ムリもない。 ‥‥ぼくだってコワい‥‥）
English: (Not that I blame her... I'm scared to death by what's going on...)

Japanese: なあ、ニイちゃん？
English: Hey, Mister.

Japanese: ‥‥あの子‥‥真宵はんや なかったよなあ‥‥？
English: That gal... Maya. She wasn't the one that pulled the trigger, was she...?

Japanese: （そうか‥‥ナツミさんは 知らないんだ‥‥）
English: (Oh, yeah...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんが霊媒をすると、 すがたまで変わってしまうこと）
English: (Lotta doesn't know that when Maya is channeling, her whole physical appearance changes.)

Japanese: でも‥‥あの部屋には ２人しかおらんかった。
English: Then again... Weren't there only the two of 'em in there?

Japanese: やっぱり、あの‥‥真宵はんが？
English: I reckon she musta done it then...

Japanese: ナツミさん。 霧崎先生のことは‥‥？
English: Lotta, what do you know about Dr. Grey?

Japanese: チョコっとやけど、 調べてるで。
English: Not much, but I did some digging...

Japanese: あんまり、評判はよくない みたいやなあ。
English: That's one gent with a bad reputation.

Japanese: そうなんですか？
English: Really?

Japanese: ウデはな、かなりイイんや。 ただ、性格がな‥‥。
English: I hear he's good at surgery and stuff, but his personality stinks like wet sheep.

Japanese: （わかるような気がする‥‥）
English: (I sort of got the same impression myself...)

Japanese: キツいんや。
English: He's real controllin'.

Japanese: 患者も看護師も、思いどおりに 動かんと、すぐにドナりちらして、
English: Soon as his nurse or patients don't do what he says, he starts a-hollerin'.

Japanese: それはもう タイヘンだったそうや。
English: Musta been real rough to work for him.

Japanese: ‥‥なぁニイちゃん。 そろそろ来るで、ケーサツ。
English: Hey, they'll be here soon, right? The police, I mean.

Japanese: アンタ、もどっておいたほうが ええんちゃう？　現場。
English: I reckon you should be gettin' back to the crime scene, now.

Japanese: （そうだな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I guess so...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 19 Kurain Village

Japanese: （目の前で殺人事件が起こって、 さすがに動揺してるみたいだな）
English: (She's probably scared out of her wits after having a murder take place right near her.)

Japanese: ナツミさん？
English: Lotta?

Japanese: ぎゃわわあっ！
English: GAWDDON'TLETITBEMENEXT!

Japanese: うおっ！ お、おどかさないでくださいよ！
English: Eek! Don't scare me like that!

Japanese: なにゆうとるんやッ！ ビックリしたのはこっちや！
English: What do ya mean!? Yer the one scaring me!

Japanese: これがウチじゃなかったら、死体が もう１個、ころがっとるところや！
English: If I weren't so tough, you'd have another dead body on yer hands -- mine!

Japanese: ナツミさん。何か気づいたこと、 ありません？
English: Did you see or notice anything that stuck out at you?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 １つだけ、あるで。
English: Nah... Well, maybe one thing...

Japanese: なんですかッ！
English: What is it!?

Japanese: ウチなぁ、意外とこーゆう話、 ニガテやったんやねえ。
English: Turns out my poor stomach's not good at handlin' this kind of thing.

Japanese: ‥‥は、はあ？
English: Huh-what?

Japanese: タタリとかオバケの復讐とか‥‥。 もうタクサンや。やめやめ。
English: Curses and ghosts and stuff comin' back to get ya... This is just too much, I say.

Japanese: な、何がですか？
English: What is too much?

Japanese: オカルトがらみのカメラマン。
English: Being an occult photographer.

Japanese: よく考えたら、めっちゃコワいわ。 今日から芸能カメラマンに転向や。
English: That's some scary stuff, so I think I'm gonna try being a celebrity photographer, now.

Japanese: ‥‥スクープ写真はモノにできず、 ってコトですか。
English: Going after photos more within your reach from now on? More glamour, less gore?

Japanese: ‥‥どうしました？
English: What's up?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: Oh, yeah!

Japanese: ど‥‥どれですか？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: ついに、どデカいスクープを モノにしたったでぇ！
English: I took some hot pictures earlier!

Japanese: えっ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ウチ、そういえば、あの現場で 押しとったやん！　シャッター！
English: Remember!? I took some pictures at the murder scene inside!

Japanese: （そ、そうだった‥‥！ しかも、２回も！）
English: (Ack! She did! Two even!)

Japanese: 霊媒中に起こった殺人‥‥ だ、大スクープやッ！
English: "The Séance Murder"... I sure like the ring of that. It'll be a sensational story!

Japanese: な‥‥ナツミさん！
English: L-Lotta!

Japanese: ゴメン！　ジャーナリストの血が 煮えたぎってきたわ！
English: Sorry, but my journalistic sense is burnin' inside me!

Japanese: 先にあやまっとくワ。 ごめんなぁ！
English: Hate to cut and run, but I'm headin' back in!

Japanese: ウチ、タレこむわ、ケーサツに。
English: Gotta beat those cops to the scoop of the century!

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおおお
English: UWAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHAHAHA HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

Japanese: おおおおおぉぉぉぉぉぉぉぉ‥‥‥
English: HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAhaha hahahahahahahahaha...

Japanese: （ま、まいったな‥‥）
English: (I don't know who's scarier... Lotta or the spirits...)

Japanese: （また、この前の事件と同じ テンカイになっちまった‥‥）
English: (And why does it feel like we've been through this before...?)

Japanese: ‥‥知らん。 なーんも知らん。
English: Nope. Don't know nothin' about that.

Japanese: ウチ、キョーミのないモンには 目もくれないんや。
English: Things that don't catch my eye, don't catch my interest.

Japanese: （イヤなジャーナリスト‥‥）
English: (And she calls herself a journalist??)

Japanese: ひさしぶりに会ったのに、 こんなことになってもうて。
English: Must be an awful thing to see each other again and have this happen.

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、あの子が サツジンなんてなぁ。
English: Never woulda guessed that gal would end up being a murderer.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんじゃ ありませんよ！
English: That wasn't Maya!

Japanese: 彼女はただ、 霊にあやつられて‥‥
English: She was being controlled by a spirit...

Japanese: ケーサツがそれ、 信じてくれるて思うか？
English: Come on, now, City Boy. Ya really think the cops'll believe that?

Japanese: お。霧崎センセやな。 キノドクなこっちゃなあ。
English: Hey, it's that Dr. Grey guy. Pity what happened to him.

Japanese: やっぱり、オバケはコワいわ。 くわばらくわばら。
English: Pretty scary what ghouls and ghosts can do to a man, huh? Gawd rest his poor soul.

Japanese: （うーん。ナツミさん、 コシが引けてるなあ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Since when was Lotta so religious...?)

Japanese: あのオバハン、 ハクリョクあったなあ。
English: That old lady sure can be forceful.

Japanese: ちょっぴりビビったワ。 ‥‥ま、ほんのちょっぴりだけど。
English: She scared me back there. ...Well, only a little, now, ya hear?

Japanese: たしか、この前の事件でも 見かけたカオやな。
English: I swear I've seen his face before. Maybe during that last case...

Japanese: 事件が起こると かならずおるよな、この刑事。
English: Whenever something happens, this detective guy is always there.

Japanese: なんや、ホンマは犯人ちゃうんか？ コイツ。
English: I wonder if he's the real baddie behind everything?

Japanese: （‥‥そのうち、ぼくまで 疑われそうだな‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I highly doubt it...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 20 Kurain Village

Japanese: （‥‥なんとしても、 真宵ちゃんを助けるんだ！）
English: (I have to save Maya, no matter what!)

Japanese: （きのう、あの部屋では いったい、何があったのか？）
English: (It's her life on the line!)

Japanese: （‥‥これからが本番だな！）
English: (What in the world happened in that room yesterday?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 20 Kurain Village

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ん？　どうしたの？
English: Hmm? What's wrong?

Japanese: わたくし、倉院の里から 出たことがありません。
English: I have never left Kurain Village.

Japanese: へ、へええ。 それは‥‥すごいね。
English: R-Really? Wow, that's, um, pretty amazing...

Japanese: これから、会いに行くのですね？ ‥‥真宵さまに。
English: You are going to meet with Mystic Maya... aren't you?

Japanese: わたくしのこと‥‥、 よろしくお伝えください。
English: Please tell her I said hi.

Japanese: あ、待って‥‥
English: Hey, wait!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥。のどかさんの ヒミツはわかったけど‥‥）
English: (She ran off... Again. Well, I know Ini's secret now, but...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんを救い出す方法なんて ゼンゼン見えてこない）
English: (I still don't have any idea how I'm going to save Maya.)

Japanese: （どんなカオして 会えばいいんだろう‥‥）
English: (I don't even know if I should go in there with a smile or a straight face... *sigh*)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: あ、成歩堂さん。 よく来てくれました！
English: Ah, Mr. Wright. I'm so glad you showed.

Japanese: 今日はいい天気で よかったですね。
English: Some nice weather we're having today. Aren't you happy?

Japanese: いや、まったくです。
English: No, not at all.

Japanese: ま、天気予報のお姉さんは またハズしてましたがね。
English: That stupid weather girl made the wrong call again.

Japanese: 《今日はドシャぶりでしょう》 スズしいカオして言ってましたよ。
English: "It will rain cats and dogs today!" she said with a straight face.

Japanese: あんなガセ情報を、あろうことか 公共の電波でお茶の間に‥‥
English: I can't believe they allow such misguided reports on the public airwaves!

Japanese: （‥‥この先生に天気の話題は タブーだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like talking about the weather with this guy is just asking for punishment...)

Japanese: ‥‥いや、それにしても リッパな屋敷ですな。
English: ...But anyway, this is a splendid manor.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ここが 真宵ちゃんの家とは‥‥）
English: (No argument there... It's hard to believe that this is Maya's house, though...)

Japanese: 屋敷の見取り図を もらってきましたよ。
English: I was given a guidemap to this building.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんもどうぞ。
English: Here, Mr. Wright. I have a copy for you too.

Japanese: 《上面図》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Guidemap added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ミゴトな筆さばきで ４つの文字がならんでいる。
English: Someone with a lot of skill wrote these four Chinese characters with a brush.

Japanese: これは‥‥“一期一会”か。
English: Oh look, here's a translation: "Once in a Lifetime."

Japanese: ‥‥霊との出会いを大切に しましょう、ということかな。
English: Its message is people should value their ghostly meetings as once in a lifetime chances.

Japanese: 霊を呼び出す“対面の間”に 通じるトビラだ。
English: A door that leads to the Channeling Chamber, the place where spirits and people meet.

Japanese: 鉄ワクで強化されていて、 ものすごくがんじょうだ。
English: The iron in this door was tempered, making it very solid.

Japanese: どうやら、カギもかかるように なっているらしい。
English: It looks like there is a strong lock on this door to top it off.

Japanese: もめんの布地に、きれいな 文字がビッシリ並んでいる。
English: A piece of cloth with a ton of finely-written characters jammed onto it.

Japanese: 霊媒師の心得みたいなものかな。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Probably esoteric knowledge only mediums would know. ...Hmm, let's see...

Japanese: タイトルは‥‥ “お金のたまる１００の方法”
English: Here's one in English... It says... "100 Ways to Save Money".

Japanese: 霊媒師の生活は、 やはりラクではないみたいだ。
English: Being a medium sounds like a rough way of life...

Japanese: こっちは わたりろうかに通じている。
English: This door leads out to the Winding Way.

Japanese: そとはいい天気みたいだな。
English: The weather outside looks really nice.

Japanese: 看護師さんの霊に会って、 どうするつもりなんですか？
English: What are you going to do once the nurse's spirit has been called?

Japanese: 決まってます。 念書を書かせるんですよ。
English: Isn't it obvious? I'm going to have her write a signed confession.

Japanese: ねんしょ？
English: A signed confession?

Japanese: そう、念書です。 いいですか、こう書かせるんです。
English: Yes, a confession. Pay attention! I'll have her write this:

Japanese: 《１年前の５月２日》
English: "One year ago, on May 2nd,

Japanese: 《私の不注意で、１４人の患者を 死なせてしまいました》
English: 14 patients died due to my negligence.

Japanese: 《そして、同月２４日》
English: And then, on May 24th,

Japanese: 《これまた自分の不注意で、 居眠り運転による交通事故死》
English: I fell asleep at the wheel and died in a car accident due to my further negligence.

Japanese: 《不注意で どうもすみませんでした》
English: I'm sorry and I apologize for being negligent."

Japanese: ‥‥いかがです！
English: That's what I'll have her write!

Japanese: これで私の心は もう梅雨あけですぞ！
English: With a confession, the rain falling on my soul can finally stop.

Japanese: そ、そうですか‥‥。
English: O-Oh, I see...

Japanese: あの。綾里　真宵のことは、 どなたから‥‥？
English: So, how did you hear about Maya Fey...?

Japanese: 私の知り合いにね。大学でオカルト を研究してる女の子がいるんです。
English: I heard about her from an acquaintance; a girl studying the occult at the college.

Japanese: その子に、この里を紹介して もらったワケですよ。
English: That girl introduced me to this village.

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の総本山 ということで。
English: This is the home of the Kurain School of Channeling.

Japanese: そうほんざん‥‥
English: The home, huh...?

Japanese: そして真宵さんは、 家元のムスメさんですからね。
English: And Ms. Maya is a daughter of the "Master", I hear.

Japanese: いえもと‥‥
English: Master...?

Japanese: ほら、そこに見えるトビラ。 あそこが霊と対面する部屋です。
English: Anyway, do you see that door? That's the room where people and spirits meet.

Japanese: 真宵さんのおばさんにあたる 人がいます。会ってきてみては？
English: Ms. Maya's aunt is in there right now. Would you care to meet her...?

Japanese: ほら、これ。 弁護士のバッジですよ。
English: Look, here's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: なんですか？　そんなモノで 私と張りあうつもりですか？
English: Are you trying to compete with me and my profession with that piece of scrap?

Japanese: 私は外科医です。 弁護士なんてメじゃない！
English: I am a surgeon. Compared to you lawyers, I'm practically a saint!

Japanese: たしかに今、患者はいない！ それはジジツです。
English: Yes, well, I don't exactly have any patients right now,

Japanese: しかし、いいですか！ ミスをしたのは私じゃないんだ！
English: but, and listen good! I'm not the one that made the mistake!

Japanese: （なんなんだよ このセンセイは‥‥）
English: (This doctor needs his head examined...)

Japanese: それにしても、 すごい屋敷ですよね。
English: Wow, this manor is really nice, don't you think?

Japanese: ‥‥このテイドで、私に勝ったと 思わないことですな。
English: Do you honestly think this place is all that impressive? Don't make me laugh.

Japanese: は？
English: Huh?

Japanese: こんなイナカ、 土地だって安い。
English: This is the countryside. Land here is dirt cheap.

Japanese: 私だって、いつかきっと 一戸建てを！
English: I will have something much greater one day! I will build my own grand mansion!

Japanese: いいですか！　一戸建てですぞ！ 見返してやりますとも！
English: Did you hear me!? A grand MANSION! This manor will seem like nothing!

Japanese: （コブシがぶるぶる ふるえている‥‥）
English: (Uh, could you keep that shaking fist away from my face? Thanks...)

Japanese: こう見えてもね。 私はいそがしいのです。
English: Look, Mr. Wright. I'm a very busy man.

Japanese: つまらんモノを 見ているヒマはないですな。
English: Don't bother me with trivial things. I simply do not have the time.

Japanese: （こんな山奥で いそがしいもクソもあるかよ）
English: (How can you be busy way up here on an isolated mountainside!?)

Japanese: ‥‥たいしたもんですな、 まだお若いのに。
English: She's still young, but one day, she'll bear a big responsibility.

Japanese: 家元ですよ、イエモト。
English: She'll be the Master, you know. The MASTER!

Japanese: （霊媒にも家元が あるとはね‥‥）
English: (I guess there are "Master" mediums, too...)

Japanese: 真宵さんのおばさんですね。
English: That's Ms. Maya's aunt.

Japanese: さっき、ムリヤリ巨大な 大福を食べさせられましたよ。
English: Earlier, she made me eat some sort of traditional dessert the size of a tea saucer!

Japanese: ‥‥ご自分では、霊媒は やらないのでしょうかねえ？
English: Hmm, I wonder if Ms. Morgan ever performs channelings herself...?

Japanese: 先生。どうですか、この人物‥‥。
English: What do you have to say about this person, Dr. Grey?

Japanese: ‥‥このカオ‥‥。
English: ...This person... Hmm... Judging by their face...

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: W-What is it?

Japanese: 胃が弱そうですな。
English: They have a weak stomach.

Japanese: そ、そうですか。
English: R-Really...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: ではこれより、倉院流霊媒術を 始めたいと存じます。
English: We will now begin the channeling.

Japanese: 術者と依頼者は、あちらの 《対面の間》にお入りください。
English: Mystic Maya and honored guest, please proceed into the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: これで世間の連中をぎゃふんと 言わせてやれますよ！
English: With this, I can finally swat all those pesky flies, once and for all!

Japanese: いいですか？　いまどき “ぎゃふん”ですぞ。
English: You hear me!? They won't be able to say a single bad thing about me after this!

Japanese: 聞きたいでしょう？ ねえ、成歩堂さん！
English: You would love to hear them apologize, wouldn't you? Wouldn't you, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: え、ぼくですか！ も、もちろん聞いてみたいです。
English: Me!? Oh, uh, yeah, I sure would.

Japanese: それでは、真宵さま。 《対面の間》のカギはお持ちに？
English: Mystic Maya, do you have the Channeling Chamber key?

Japanese: あ。持ったよ、ホラ。
English: Yup, right here.

Japanese: よろしゅうござあます。
English: That is most satisfactory.

Japanese: １個しかないカギですからね、 気をつけてくださいまし。
English: That key is one-of-a-kind, so please take care of it well.

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ、おばさま。 なくさないって！
English: It'll be fine, Aunt Morgan. I won't lose it, trust me!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、霧崎先生。 行きましょうか。
English: OK, Dr. Grey. Let's get started.

Japanese: ハイ。まいりましょう！
English: Finally! Let's go!

Japanese: ‥‥ガチャリ‥‥
English: *click*

Japanese: では、アタクシどもは、 ここでお待ちいたしましょう。
English: Now let us wait patiently out here for them.

Japanese: それはそれはニガいお茶と、アゴが はずれるほどアマい大福を‥‥
English: Please have some lovely bitter green tea and jaw-droppingly large strawberry desserts...

Japanese: ちょっと、オバハン！
English: Hold on, now, granny!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥オバハン？
English: ...Granny?

Japanese: なんでウチらは部屋に 入られへんねん！
English: How come we ain't allowed in that room!?

Japanese: ああた、アレでござあますわねえ。 とても日本語がおじょうずな‥‥
English: Dear madam, you have an "impressive" grasp of English. From where did you learn it?

Japanese: ウチはネっからのナニワっ子や！
English: What!? I'm from the heart of the heartland!

Japanese: ならばその、 ナニワとやらにお帰りなさい。
English: Is that so? Then I humbly request that you return to this "Heart of the Heartland".

Japanese: な‥‥なんや‥‥？
English: Wh-What are ya...?

Japanese: ここは倉院の里。
English: This is Kurain Village.

Japanese: しきたりに従えない人間は、 出ていくことでござあます。
English: If you can not follow our traditions and rules, we request that you leave!

Japanese: ‥‥うう。ス、スンマセン。
English: Sorry.

Japanese: （うわあ‥‥あのナツミさんを ヘコませたぞ‥‥すげえ！）
English: (Wow. She shut Lotta up! Now THAT's impressive!)

Japanese: では。 ‥‥お待ちいたしましょう。
English: Now then, let us wait.

Japanese: バァァァン！
English: *BANG*

Japanese: うわあッ！ ‥‥なな、なんや今の音！
English: Hey now! Wh-What was that just now!?

Japanese: （《対面の間》の中から 聞こえたぞ‥‥）
English: (It came from inside the Channeling Chamber...)

Japanese: バァァァン！
English: *BANG*

Japanese: ニイちゃん！　コレ、銃声やで！
English: Hey! Th-That's a gunshot!

Japanese: な、なんでござあますって！
English: E-Excuse me!?

Japanese: 前にも聞いたことがあるんや。 マチガイない！　銃声や！
English: I've heard that sound before. It's a gunshot! I'd bet my afro on it!

Japanese: （な‥‥中には真宵ちゃんが！）
English: (M-Maya! Maya's in there!)

Japanese: どうする気や！　ニイちゃん！
English: Well, Mister Lawyer! What's the plan!?

Japanese: もちろん、 あの部屋に入るんですよ！
English: What else!? We're going to break in!

Japanese: え！　で、でも、 １個しかないカギは、あの子が‥‥
English: What!? But... But there's only one key and that kid's got it.

Japanese: トビラごと、ぶっコワします。 いいですね！　キミ子さん。
English: We're going to have to break the door. Is that alright, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: え‥‥で、でも、 アレでござあますねえ、その‥‥
English: Well, yes... But there is one matter... That is...

Japanese: 止めてもムダです。
English: Don't try to stop me!

Japanese: 修理費の請求書は、 成歩堂法律事務所へどうぞ！
English: You can send the repair bill to the Wright & Co. Law Offices later!

Japanese: ええでッ！ オトコやナルホドー！
English: Yeah! Now that's what I call being a man!

Japanese: ‥‥ドカッ！　‥‥ドカッ！ ‥‥バァン！
English: *SLAM* *SLAM* *CRASH!*

Japanese: キミ子さん‥‥どうしましょう！
English: Ms. Fey! What should we do!?

Japanese: なにノンキなコト言ってるんや！ さっさと踏みこむんや！
English: Yellow-bellied chicken liver! What are ya waiting for!? Kick it down already!

Japanese: で‥‥でも、カギが‥‥
English: But... The key...

Japanese: カギは１個しかござあません。
English: That key is one-of-a-kind. In this world, there is only that one.

Japanese: アタクシでも、あのトビラを 開けることはできません。
English: Even I am powerless to open that door.

Japanese: （‥‥そういえばそうだったな！） わかりました。
English: (Hmm, there is really no other way...) Alright.

Japanese: どいてください！ トビラをぶっコワします！
English: Stand aside! I'm going to break the door down!

Japanese: ちょっと‥‥お待ちなさい！ そんな、乱暴な‥‥。
English: W-Wait! Please! That sort of recklessness...

Japanese: ええでッ！ オトコやナルホドー！
English: Yeah! Now that's what I call being a man!

Japanese: ‥‥ドカッ！　‥‥ドカッ！ ‥‥バァン！
English: *SLAM* *SLAM* *CRASH!*

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: ああ。成歩堂さん。
English: Ah, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: キミ子さん！ あの、真宵‥‥さま、は？
English: Ms. Fey! How's, um... Mystic... Maya?

Japanese: “こちら側”にもどられました。 除霊の術をほどこしましたので。
English: She has returned to "this world". The Spirit Severing Technique was successful.

Japanese: 会わせてください！
English: Let me see her, please!

Japanese: 今は気を失っておられます。 そっとしておいてくださいまし。
English: She is still unconscious. I humbly request that you wait outside for her recovery.

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (Guess I just have to wait... *sigh*)

Japanese: お待たせしたッス！　警察ッス！
English: The police are here! Sorry to keep you waiting!

Japanese: あ、あれ。‥‥またアンタッスか！
English: Huh? ...You again!?

Japanese: なんでイトノコ刑事が こんなところに‥‥？
English: What are you doing all the way out here, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: たまたまッス。 出張でこっちに来てたッス。
English: It's actually kinda funny. I was in the area for a business trip, pal.

Japanese: では、これから現場を調べるッス。
English: Well, time to check out the crime scene.

Japanese: みなさん、のちほど取り調べを するッスから、
English: I'll have to question everyone here later on,

Japanese: ここから動いちゃダメッスよ！
English: so just sit tight, alright?

Japanese: （なんでぼくの目を 見つめて言うんだよ‥‥） me...?)
English: (Why is he grinning at

Japanese: じゃあ、イトノコ刑事さん。 現場にご案内いたしましょう。
English: Shall I show you to the scene of the crime, Detective Gymshoe?

Japanese: いや、あの‥‥。 自分はイトノコではないッス‥‥
English: Um... My name isn't Gymshoe...

Japanese: （２人は《対面の間》に 入ってしまった）
English: (The two of them went into the Channeling Chamber...)

Japanese: （しかたない、みんなの 話でも聞いてまわるかな‥‥）
English: (I guess all I can do for now is see what everyone else has to say...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: あら、ああた。 ‥‥ちょうどよかったですわ。
English: Oh, dear madam... You have such impeccable timing.

Japanese: なんかあ、 お話があるそうですよお。
English: Like, what's all the buzz?

Japanese: えー、みなさん。 聞いてほしいッス！
English: Ahem. Alright everyone, listen up! You too, pal!

Japanese: これから事情聴取をおこなうッス！
English: I'm going to give you all a brief update!

Japanese: たぶん、終わるのは夜中に なりそうッスけど‥‥。
English: Right now, it looks like the investigation's not going to be done until real late...

Japanese: やむをえませんわねえ。
English: That is a terrible shame.

Japanese: 今夜はみなさん、綾里のお屋敷に お泊まりいただきましょう。
English: I propose that everyone sleep here under the roof of the Fey Manor tonight.

Japanese: （まったく、ヤッカイなことに なったもんだ‥‥）
English: (What a mess. I can't believe how crazy this day turned out...)

Japanese: （どうやら真宵ちゃんは、 警察署に連行されたらしい）
English: (Maya was arrested and taken away by the police.)

Japanese: （‥‥その夜は、 眠れるはずもなかった‥‥）
English: (And I don't see myself getting any sleep tonight...)

Japanese: ６月２０日　午前　８時　２分 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 20, 8:02 AM Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: （‥‥う‥‥朝か‥‥いつのまにか 眠っていたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (*yawn* Uuugh... Morning... I guess I must have nodded off at some point...)

Japanese: （とにかく、真宵ちゃんが心配だ。 すぐ留置所に行こう！）
English: (I hope Maya's OK. I should hurry and get to the Detention Center, ASAP!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 20 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: （“修験者の間”というぐらいだ。 いつもは霊媒師のタマゴたちが）
English: (The Meditation Room nurtures all mediums young and old.)

Japanese: （修行でもしてるんだろう。 今日は、がらんとしているな）
English: (Even mediums in training, I'd wager. But it's pretty deserted today...)

Japanese: （きのうの殺人事件の せいだろうな‥‥）
English: (...probably because of the murder yesterday...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 20 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: おっ！　アンタ。 やはり、彼女の弁護を？
English: Hey, it's you, pal! So you're going to be her lawyer, I bet.

Japanese: そういうことです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: そッスか‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: 今度ばかりは、自分も アンタの味方をしたいッス。
English: I really feel bad for you, pal. Just this once, I wish I could be on your side.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: ま、そういうワケにも いかないッスがね。
English: Yeah, but I shouldn't be saying that kind of stuff to you.

Japanese: おっ！　かわいい女の子ッスね。
English: Hey! You're a cute kid.

Japanese: ハッハッハッ。 自分はコワくないッスよー。
English: Ha ha ha. I'm not scary. Honest!

Japanese: （へええ。イトノコさん、 子ども好きなんだな‥‥）
English: (Wow, I never knew Gumshoe liked kids...)

Japanese: 今、イイもの見せてあげるッス。 ホンモノのピストルを‥‥。
English: H-Hey! I know! I'll show you something cool! How's this? It's a real, genuine pistol...

Japanese: イ、イトノコ刑事。 よけいなコトしないでください！
English: D-Detective Gumshoe!! What are you doing showing her something that dangerous!

Japanese: うう‥‥。 こりゃどうも、すまねッス。
English: Oh, sorry...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの審理、 明日なんですよね‥‥。
English: About Maya's trial tomorrow...

Japanese: アンタには、 ニュースが２つあるッス。
English: I've got two pieces of news for you, pal.

Japanese: ニュースが‥‥２つ？
English: Two?

Japanese: “よくないニュース”と、 “もっとよくないニュース”ッス。
English: Yeah. "Bad news" and "even worse news."

Japanese: どっちを先に聞きたいッス？
English: So which do you want to hear first?

Japanese: ‥‥どっちでもいいです。
English: ...I don't really care... It doesn't change the fact that I'm not going to like it.

Japanese: 明日の法廷は、 あの狩魔検事が担当するッス。
English: Alright, pal. Well, the prosecutor for the trial is Prosecutor von Karma.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！ カルマ検事‥‥？
English: WHAT!? Von Karma!?

Japanese: （狩魔　豪（かるまごう）‥‥ 奇跡のベテラン検事）
English: (Manfred von Karma... He was an awe-inspiring veteran prosecutor.)

Japanese: （４０年間、無敗をほこり、 恐るべき弟子を育て上げた男）
English: (He never lost a case in the 40 years of his career, and he raised a fearsome disciple.)

Japanese: （タチの悪さと後味の悪さは、 一生忘れないだろう‥‥）
English: (The horrible aftertaste of the evil he force-fed me is something I'll never forget.)

Japanese: あれ？
English: Wait a sec.

Japanese: でも、あの人はもう、 法廷に立てる状況では‥‥
English: But didn't Von Karma stand trial and...

Japanese: それが、もう１つの ワルいニュースッス。
English: That's where the other piece of bad news comes in...

Japanese: アンタが戦う狩魔検事は、 その２代目ッス。
English: The Von Karma you're facing tomorrow is actually his successor...

Japanese: に‥‥にだいめ‥‥？
English: Successor...?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの容疑‥‥、 どうなってますか？
English: What about Maya and the charges against her?

Japanese: アンタに勝ち目はないッス。 証拠なら、いくらでもあるッス。
English: I don't think you can win, pal. If you're talking about proof, we've got a few pieces.

Japanese: 証拠が‥‥？
English: You've got proof...?

Japanese: 明日は、あの大阪弁の カメラマンが証言するッス。
English: Yeah, pal. That puffy-haired photographer's going to testify tomorrow.

Japanese: （ナツミさん、か。 そういえばきのう‥‥）
English: (He must be talking about Lotta. Actually... speaking of Lotta...)

Japanese: ついに、どデカいスクープを モノにしたったでぇ！
English: I took some hot pictures earlier!

Japanese: えっ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ウチ、そういえば、あの現場で 押しとったやん！　シャッター！
English: Remember!? I took some pictures at the murder scene inside!

Japanese: （ナツミさんの“スクープ写真” ‥‥何が写ってるんだ‥‥？）
English: (Lotta's "hot pictures"... I wonder what her camera captured...?)

Japanese: ‥‥それに、アンタだって わかってるはずッス。
English: ...Besides, you must've realized by now, pal.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵以外のだれも、 あの犯行は不可能だったッス。
English: There's no way anyone other than Maya Fey could've done it.

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、何か言いたげに ぼくを見上げている‥‥）
English: (What am I supposed to say to Pearls now...?)

Japanese: 霧崎先生のことですけど‥‥。
English: About Dr. Grey...

Japanese: 被害者は、ちょっとした 有名人だったッス。
English: The victim wasn't super famous, but people still knew who he was.

Japanese: １年ぐらい前、ワイドショーで さわがれていたッス。
English: His face was all over those tabloid shows last year.

Japanese: ああ、それなら知ってますよ。
English: Yeah, I know.

Japanese: １年前の新聞を、いろいろ 探してみたッス。
English: I was looking through some newspapers from last year, and...

Japanese: あれ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 記事の切り抜きを‥‥ どこへやったッスかねえ‥‥。
English: I could've sworn I had... I cut an article out, but I guess I must've lost it...

Japanese: （ポケットを探してるよ‥‥。 よっぽど見たいらしいな）
English: (He's searching through his pockets... And boy, are those big...)

Japanese: うう‥‥どうしても 気になるッス。見たいッス。
English: Hmm... It looked really interesting, too.

Japanese: アンタ、持ってないッスか？ ‥‥その記事。
English: Hey, do you have a copy, pal?

Japanese: （新聞記事‥‥か）
English: (A copy of that article...?)

Japanese: （たしか、霧崎先生が事務所に 持ってきていたような‥‥）
English: (I think Dr. Grey might have brought a copy to the office when he came by...)

Japanese: あの‥‥狩魔検事の ２代目、っていうのは‥‥？
English: So, um... Who is this "successor" to Von Karma?

Japanese: そのまんまッス。 狩魔検事のお子さんッス。
English: Literally, pal, it's Prosecutor von Karma's kid.

Japanese: １３才という、異例の若さで 検事になって以来、負け知らず。
English: His kid became a prosecutor real young... like 13, and hasn't lost a trial since.

Japanese: ‥‥まさに“天才”ッス。
English: That's what they call a "prodigy," pal.

Japanese: へえ‥‥‥。って、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hmm... Kid, huh... W-W-Wait a sec!

Japanese: じゅ、１３才で検事ですって！
English: Th-Th-Thirteen!? The kid became a prosecutor at the age of THIRTEEN!?

Japanese: “異例”とか“天才”とか、 そういうモンダイでは‥‥
English: I mean, a prodigy like that... I would have heard something about...

Japanese: いやいや。２代目はアメリカで 生まれ育ったッスから。
English: Oh, no, no. The kid was born and raised in Germany, pal.

Japanese: 自由の国では、才能しだいで なんでもありッス。
English: That country's got a lot of stuff and is a great place to develop a person's talents.

Japanese: （アメリカ、か。どうりで 聞いたことがないと思ったよ）
English: (Germany, huh? That's probably why I've never heard of this person.)

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥カルマという 名前を聞くと‥‥）
English: (Ugh... Still, all it takes is someone mentioning the name "Von Karma",)

Japanese: （‥‥どうしても、あの事件を 思い出してしまう‥‥）
English: (and I get terrible flashbacks to that case.)

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！　いいかげんに、 もう忘れないと‥‥）
English: (Stop it! Get over it, Phoenix!)

Japanese: （‥‥アイツはもう いないんだから‥‥）
English: (That person is already gone...)

Japanese: ほら。弁護士のバッジです。
English: Hey, look. It's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: はっはっ。あいかわらず サエないバッジッスね。
English: Ha ha. Good one, pal. You're always good for a laugh with your little badge.

Japanese: やっぱり男は、 ダマって警察手帳ッス。
English: But a real man... A real man has a police badge.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あの。なるほどくん？
English: Uh, um... Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ん？
English: What is it, Pearls?

Japanese: わたくしも、何かお見せしたほうが よろしいのでしょうか？
English: I was wondering if I could see it, too?

Japanese: い、いや。ベツに 気を使わなくていいよ。
English: Ah, no, it's OK. It's not that important anyway.

Japanese: あの‥‥これ。 被害者が持ってきたんですけど。
English: Um, Dr. Grey brought this over to my office the other day, and...

Japanese: おお！　それそれ。 医療ミスッス。
English: Hey! It's that news story! The one about the malpractice suit!

Japanese: 患者が１４人も亡くなって、 ずいぶんさわがれましたよね。
English: Fourteen patients died and it caused a huge stink.

Japanese: しかも、この事件の直後、 さらに事故が重なったッスから。
English: Yeah, but that's not all, pal. Things only got worse after that.

Japanese: 看護師さんが亡くなった 交通事故ですか。
English: You mean the car accident the nurse died in?

Japanese: ‥‥おッ、あったあった。
English: ...Hmm... Oh, here we go.

Japanese: 自分は、その事故の 新聞記事を持ってるッス。
English: I brought my own clipping with me today.

Japanese: よかったら、この記事、 持って行くッス。
English: Here, pal, this is for you.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 じゃ、もらっておきます。
English: Alright. Thanks.

Japanese: 証拠品《新聞記事・２》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Newspaper Clipping 2 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 患者は死なせるは、看護師は 事故死するは。
English: The nurse that killed the patients ended up getting killed in an accident.

Japanese: 被害者はワイドショーに 引っぱりだこッス。
English: Dr. Grey wound up on a lot of tabloid shows because of that.

Japanese: いい宣伝になったッスね、 きっと。
English: Now, there was a smart man. I'm sure that was a great way to advertise for his clinic.

Japanese: （おかげで患者がぱったり 来なくなったらしいからな）
English: (Uh, huh. Scaring people off is always the best way to get more patients.)

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥。
English: What do you think about this...?

Japanese: おっ。キレイな ブローチッスねえ。
English: Hey, that's a pretty nice- looking brooch!

Japanese: あんなきたない 弁護士バッジなんかやめて、
English: You should stop wearing that ugly old attorney's badge,

Japanese: それ、ぶらさげとくッス。
English: and start wearing that, pal.

Japanese: ふふ。よかったですね、 なるほどくん。
English: Hee, hee. That detective is so nice! Aren't you happy, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （よかったって‥‥何が？）
English: (Happy?... About what?)

Japanese: あの‥‥このカギ なんですけど。
English: So about this key...

Japanese: なんスか？　アンタの ウチのカギッスか？
English: Hey, is that your house key or something, pal?

Japanese: そんなワケないでしょう！
English: Come on, does this look like a house key to you?

Japanese: じゃ、どこのカギッスか？
English: Then what's it for?

Japanese: それは‥‥
English: It's...

Japanese: （‥‥こいつは、ヒミツに しておいたほうがいいか）
English: (Hmm, actually, I should probably keep that a secret...)

Japanese: ぼくの自転車のカギです。
English: It's my bicycle key.

Japanese: なあんだ、そッスか。
English: Oh. Nothing special, huh?

Japanese: おおっとォ。 それはダメッスね。
English: Ooh, I'd better shut my mouth.

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: Uh, what?

Japanese: 証拠品に関する情報は あげられないッス。
English: I'm not gonna give you any information about evidence, pal.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Oh.

Japanese: うーん。こうして見ると、 アイソのないツラッスねえ。
English: Boy do I look scary in this picture.

Japanese: もっと、市民に親しまれる 刑事を目ざすべきッスね。
English: I guess if I want people to stop thinking cops are scary, I have to change myself.

Japanese: スマイルスマイル。
English: Gotta smile, gotta smile!

Japanese: （にたにたとブキミに ほほえみ始めた‥‥）
English: (There's that classic Gumshoe goofy smile again...)

Japanese: ああ。綾里　真宵の おばさんッスね。
English: That's Maya's aunt, right?

Japanese: さっき、おいしい 大福をもらったッス。
English: I got to eat one of her giant strawberry desserts earlier. It was really good!

Japanese: （そういえば、ぼくは 食べてないな‥‥）
English: (Aw, I didn't get to eat one yet...)

Japanese: このたびは、ホントに おキノドクッス。
English: I'm sorry things had to turn out this way, pal.

Japanese: ま、まだ有罪と決まった ワケじゃありませんよ！
English: It's too early to give up! No one's proven her guilty of anything, yet!

Japanese: いくらアンタでも、 今度ばかりは‥‥。
English: Look, even if there were a zillion of you, pal, this is a case you...

Japanese: ‥‥はあぁ。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: （タメ息をつかれた‥‥）
English: (Now why is he sighing?)

Japanese: ‥‥ははあ。その子が 春美ちゃんッスか。
English: Oh, so that's little Pearl.

Japanese: 何か知ってるんですか？
English: Do you know her?

Japanese: さっき、キミ子さんが さがしていたッス。
English: Only that Ms. Fey was looking for her earlier.

Japanese: ヘンな男につきまとわれてる とか、シンパイしてたッス。
English: Something about being worried that she'll end up talking to the weird guy.

Japanese: アンタ、その男について 心当たり、あるッスか？
English: So, you have any idea who this "weird guy" is, pal?

Japanese: ‥‥いえ。ベツに。
English: ...Nope. Not a clue.

Japanese: その人物に関する 情報は、あげられないッス。
English: I don't have anything to tell you about this one, pal.

Japanese: ま、そんな カタいこと言わずに。
English: You don't have to be so mean about it.

Japanese: ダーメーッースゥ。
English: Not. A. Word.

Japanese: そそそ、そんな目で 見ちゃイヤッス！
English: H-H-Hey! Don't look at me like that!

Japanese: （ホネまでしみるんだよなあ‥‥ 春美ちゃんの視線）
English: (That girl has one intense stare... *gulp*)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・対面の間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Channeling Chamber

Japanese: （うわあ‥‥ なんてまあ、アヤシイ‥‥）
English: (Wow... It's such a strange atmosphere in here...)

Japanese: （このユラユラした明かりは‥‥ ローソク、みたいだな）
English: (These flickering lights... I guess they're candles...)

Japanese: ああた！ ‥‥どちらさまで？
English: Good sir! Who might you be...?

Japanese: うわ！　は、はい。 ぼくは成歩堂　龍一といって‥‥
English: Aaah! I-I'm, uh, my name is Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: ああ。 ‥‥ああたが、あの。
English: Oh... You must be that good sir...

Japanese: （“ああた”って‥‥ ぼくのコトか？）
English: ("Good sir"...? Is she talking about me?)

Japanese: ああた、ベンゴシでござあましょ？
English: You are a lawyer, are you not, good sir?

Japanese: 真宵さまから うかがっておりますわよ。
English: I have heard much about you from Mystic Maya.

Japanese: それはどうも。 （真宵“サマ”‥‥？）
English: Is that so? ("Mystic" Maya...?)

Japanese: アタクシは真宵さまのおば、 綾里　キミ子でござあます。
English: I am Mystic Maya's aunt, Morgan Fey.

Japanese: よ、よろしくおねがいします。
English: N-Nice to meet you.

Japanese: ああた、アレでござあますわねえ。
English: Ah, I had wanted to speak with you about a certain incident, good sir.

Japanese: 殿方なんですから、あまり真宵さま に頼ってばかりでは、
English: It would seem that perhaps you ask too much of Mystic Maya in performing your duties.

Japanese: みっとものうござあますよ。
English: Most unbecoming, I'm afraid.

Japanese: は？
English: Huh?

Japanese: アレでござあましょ？
English: Don't tell me you have already forgotten?

Japanese: ああたが勝訴した事件のほとんどは 真宵さまのスイリとヒラメキで‥‥
English: That was it not for Mystic Maya and her assistance, you would surly not have won...

Japanese: （‥‥女の子には走って逃げられ、 おばさんにはマユをしかめられ）
English: (First a girl that runs away, and now an old lady who says I stink at doing my job...)

Japanese: （どうも、ダメなオトコの 見本になってるな、ぼく‥‥）
English: (When did I become the poster boy for "How Not to Make a First Impression"...?)

Japanese: ま。アレでござあますわね。 もっとシャンとなさるコト。
English: The past is the past though. Let's us speak of the present now.

Japanese: ‥‥ココロがけます。
English: ...Ah, thank you...

Japanese: ああ、そういえば。
English: Oh, yes.

Japanese: ああた、春美ちゃんを 見かけませんでしたかしら？
English: I wonder if you have yet to meet Pearl, good sir.

Japanese: “はるみちゃん”‥‥？
English: Pearl?

Japanese: 天女のような、愛くるしい 女の子でござあます。
English: She is an adorable, angelic young girl.

Japanese: ああ。あのブアイソウな‥‥
English: Oh, that odd...

Japanese: アタクシのムスメでござあますが、 今、なんと‥‥？
English: She is my daughter. Now then, what was it you were about to say...?

Japanese: か‥‥ かわいいムスメさんですね。
English: Wh-What a cute daughter you have... Ahaha...

Japanese: 世間知らずで好奇心が 強いものでござあますから。
English: A pure heart that knows not of the evils in this world is a powerful thing, good sir.

Japanese: あまり相手にしない ようにしてくださいましね。
English: Please refrain from affiliating with her, I request.

Japanese: （あの子のほうが 相手にしてくれなかったけど）
English: (Well, it's not like I had the chance to anyway...)

Japanese: くれぐれも、マチガイを 起こしませぬよう。
English: I must insist and stress that you not let it occur.

Japanese: 起こしませんよ！
English: Okay, okay, I get it!

Japanese: “修験者の間”に通じる扉。 ずいぶん重そうだ。
English: The Meditation Room is on the other side of this very heavy-looking door.

Japanese: 取っ手のところに、大きな鉄の 錠前がとりつけられている。
English: There is a giant iron lock where the handle is.

Japanese: 日常の世界と霊界をへだてている ‥‥そんな感じがする。
English: It feels like a curtain that separates the normal world from the spirit one.

Japanese: 霊媒師がすわる場所だろうか。 タタミが４枚、しかれている。
English: This is where the medium sits, I assume. There are 4 panels of this flooring.

Japanese: 空気がよどんでいるせいか、 ちょっとジメジメしてるな。
English: The straw in the flooring feels a little damp, probably from the humidity in the air.

Japanese: 霊媒師がすわる場所だろうか。 タタミが４枚、しかれている。
English: This is where the medium sits, I assume. There are 4 panels of this flooring.

Japanese: 空気がよどんでいるせいか、 ちょっとジメジメしている。
English: The straw in the flooring feels a little damp, probably from the humidity in the air.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしても、血のあとに 目が行ってしまうな‥‥。
English: No matter what I do, my eyes keep getting drawn to the pool of blood in the center here.

Japanese: 祭壇‥‥というのだろうか。 重いフンイキがただよっている。
English: I guess this is an alter. Whatever it is, it feels very important.

Japanese: 表面がうすくけむった カガミのようなものや、
English: A mirror-like object with a cloudy reflective surface,

Japanese: 青あおとしたサカキが まつられている。
English: and some branches of a sacred tree have been set in a pre-determined arrangement.

Japanese: あ、ちょっとああた。 さわらないでもらえるかしら。
English: Good sir! I wonder if you would please not touch the sacred objects.

Japanese: それは念入りに おそなえしておりますから。
English: Those objects are being offered to the spirits.

Japanese: さわると、そう‥‥ タタリがござあます、タタリが。
English: If you were to touch them, good sir, they would become cursed. Yes, cursed.

Japanese: （シロートだと思って、 テキトーなこと言ってるぞ‥‥）
English: (She really didn't have to talk down to me. I'm not that much of an idiot...)

Japanese: ビョウブのうしろは 床の間になっているようだ。
English: There is some empty space behind the folding screen.

Japanese: ローソクのあかりも とどかず、まっくらだな。
English: The light of the candles can't reach back there, so it's completely dark.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、“サマ”づけで 呼ばれるほどエラいんですか？
English: So why do you add "Mystic" to Maya's name. Isn't that a little... strange?

Japanese: ああたッ！
English: Good sir!

Japanese: はいッ！
English: Yes!

Japanese: 真宵さまに向かって、なんて なれなれしい！
English: How dare you be so rude and disrespectful towards Mystic Maya!

Japanese: す‥‥すみません。
English: I-I'm sorry.

Japanese: “真宵さま”と お呼びなさいまし！
English: She must be addressed by her proper title, good sir. You must call her "Mystic Maya"!

Japanese: あ、あの。‥‥その、真宵さま ‥‥のコトですけど？
English: Um, uh... So, about Mystic Maya... Uh...

Japanese: 血‥‥でござあます。
English: It is the blood.
Excuse me? "Blood"?

Japanese: あの方は、家元の血を受けた 最後のひとりでござあます。
English: Mystic Maya carries the blood of the Master. In actuality, she is the only one remaining.
(Only one remaining...? What's that supposed to mean?)

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の正当な後継者‥‥ それが、真宵さまでござあます。
English: Mystic Maya is the last of the rightful heirs of the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥あの、 ああた‥‥いや、あなたは？
English: OK... Then... where do you stand...?

Japanese: そうでござあますね。
English: That is a little more complicated...

Japanese: アタクシども、姓は綾里ですが、 “分家”なのでござあます。
English: Although a woman of the Fey family, I am merely a member of the branch family.

Japanese: ‥‥ぶんけ？
English: ...Branch family? What's that?

Japanese: 分家の人間は、たとえどんなに 霊力が強くとも‥‥
English: It is as it sounds. For a member of the branch family, no matter her spiritual power,

Japanese: 倉院流の家元になることは できないのでござあます。
English: she can never become the Master of the Kurain School.

Japanese: あの‥‥あなたの霊力は どうなんですか？
English: So... how strong are you...?

Japanese: ザンネンでござあますが‥‥
English: It is a shame, however I will admit.

Japanese: 家元の足もとにも およびませんわね。
English: I can not even begin to compare the lowliness of my power to the Master's.

Japanese: （そんなものなのか‥‥）
English: (So that's how it is...)

Japanese: 今日の霊媒は、彼女の初仕事に なるとか？
English: So today's channeling will be her first?

Japanese: そのとおりでござあます。
English: Yes, good sir.

Japanese: 交通事故の死者は 思念が強うござあますから、
English: Because the spirit of a person who dies in a traffic accident is usually very strong,

Japanese: 呼び出すのは、たやすい コトでござあましょう。
English: it is usually easier to call that person to our world.

Japanese: （“交通事故”ねえ‥‥）
English: ("Traffic accident" indeed.)

Japanese: 霊媒はこの《対面の間》にて おこなわれますので。
English: The channeling will take place here, in this Channeling Chamber, good sir.

Japanese: アタクシがこうして今、 準備をしているのでござあます。
English: As you can see, I am in the process of preparing.

Japanese: あの‥‥この部屋は‥‥。 《対面の間》でしたっけ？
English: So... this room is the... Channeling Chamber...?

Japanese: 霊媒師が、霊と語らう 場所でござあますわ。
English: The place where a spirit medium speaks with those who have departed.

Japanese: じゃあ、ぼくたちもここで‥‥？
English: I don't suppose you'd mind if we observed the session...

Japanese: トンデモござあません。
English: Do not even think of such nonsense!

Japanese: ここに入れるのは、依頼者と 術者の２人だけでござあます。
English: Only the medium and the client may enter here.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか？
English: O-Oh.

Japanese: 倉院流の奥義を、そうそう人前で お見せするわけにはまいりません。
English: The secrets of the Kurain Techniques can not be seen by just anybody.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、ぼくの前で 見せまくっていたけどな‥‥）
English: (That never stopped Maya from doing it in front of me before...)

Japanese: 何かマチガイがあっては 困りますから、
English: It is also for your safety, good sir, if something were to go wrong.

Japanese: 交霊中は、そこのトビラに カギをかけることに‥‥。
English: That is why, while channeling is being performed, that heavy door is securely locked...

Japanese: カギ‥‥ですか。
English: Oh, that's what the lock is for...

Japanese: ああたッ！
English: Good sir!

Japanese: はいッ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ‥‥霊媒は３時から 始めたいと思います。
English: We would like to begin the channeling at 3 o'clock.

Japanese: 準備をさせていただいて よろしいかしら。
English: If you don't mind, good sir, would you mind allowing me to make preparations?

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうぞ。 （怒られてばっかりだな‥‥）
English: Uh, um, sure... go ahead... (She's a Fey alright! Just as odd as the rest.)

Japanese: ああたッ！
English: Good sir!

Japanese: は、はいッ！
English: Y-Yes!

Japanese: いつの間にそんな‥‥
English: When did you...

Japanese: 真宵さまを かくし撮りするなんて！
English: take a hidden picture of Mystic Maya!?

Japanese: そ、そ、そんなんじゃ ないですよ！
English: Wh-Wh-What!? Wait... It's not like that at all!

Japanese: ユルしませぬ！
English: It is unforgivable!

Japanese: いてて‥‥。 （ひっぱたかれた‥‥）
English: Ow-ow-ow! (Why did she slap me?)

Japanese: この人ですけど‥‥。
English: About this person...

Japanese: ああ。アレでござあましょ？
English: Oh! It is that man, is it not?

Japanese: 死者のチカラを借りて 自分の無力さをかくそうとする、
English: A man who wishes to borrow a departed spirit's power to hide the lack of his own.

Japanese: ちっぽけな殿方。
English: What an insignificant man.

Japanese: ち、ちっぽけ‥‥。
English: Insignificant...?

Japanese: ああたは、その殿方の ちっぽけなお友だち。
English: That would mean you are that man's insignificant little friend, dear sir.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは、そのぼくの ちっぽけな助手、ってところか）
English: (Then I guess that makes Maya my insignificant little assistant, huh?)

Japanese: き‥‥霧崎先生ッ！
English: D-Dr. Grey!!
*klik*

Japanese: ‥‥あたしは‥‥
あたしは‥‥殺されたの‥‥
English: ...I was... murdered...

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵‥‥ちゃん？
English: M-Maya?

Japanese: あの男は‥‥私を殺した‥‥
English: That man... ...murdered me...

Japanese: ‥‥だから‥‥殺してやった‥‥
English: ...so I killed... him...

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって‥‥！
English: Wh-What!?
*klik*

Japanese: ナツミさん！ ‥‥こんなときに‥‥
English: Lotta! At a time like this!?

Japanese: こんなときだからこそ、や！
English: Times like this are perfect for snapping up shots!

Japanese: それより、ど‥‥どういうことや！ この子‥‥真宵はん‥‥なんか？
English: But anyway... what's going on here!? This gal... Is she... ...Maya!?

Japanese: そこをどくのですッ！
English: Step away from there!

Japanese: この場はアタクシに おまかせなさい。
English: Please leave this area to me.

Japanese: ああたたちは、警察に電話を！
English: Go quickly and inform the police!
But, but...

Japanese: 早くなさいッ！ ‥‥ギセイ者がふえる前に。
English: Hurry! Before there are more victims here!

Japanese: ほれ！　行くでニイちゃん！ オバハンにまかせるんやッ！
English: Hey, let's go, City Boy! Leave this to the granny!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・対面の間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Channeling Chamber

Japanese: （警官たちがいそがしそうに 動きまわっている）
English: (The police are busily running here and there,)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんのすがたは ‥‥見えない）
English: (and I don't see Maya anywhere...)

Japanese: あ、アンタ！ 勝手に入っちゃダメッス。
English: H-Hey, you! Don't touch anything!

Japanese: 少しだけ、 話を聞かせてください！
English: Look, I really need to ask you some questions!

Japanese: うう‥‥。断りにくい目で 見つめないでほしいッス。
English: Umm... Don't look at me like I'm some sort of uncaring jerk...

Japanese: じゃ、ちょっとだけッスよ。
English: Tell you what, pal. I'll let you ask me a few questions, alright?

Japanese: あっ！ 勝手にさわっちゃダメッス！
English: Hey, pal! No touching without my permission!

Japanese: 見るだけですよ。
English: I'm just looking at it.

Japanese: 容疑者があの子だけに、 イマイチ信用できないッス！
English: Hey, I can't be too careful if a kid like her can be a murder suspect, now can I?

Japanese: （‥‥部屋を調べるのは 後にするしかないな‥‥）
English: (I guess if I want to search this room, I'm going to have to come back later...)

Japanese: あの‥‥真宵ちゃんは‥‥？
English: Um, about Maya...

Japanese: アンタには言いにくいッスが‥‥
English: I hate to break this to you, pal, but...

Japanese: 状況から、彼女が犯人であることは マチガイないッス。
English: with the way things look now, that girl's the only one who could've done it.

Japanese: （たしかに、この部屋には被害者と 彼女しかいなかったからな‥‥）
English: (Yeah. Only Maya and the victim were inside this room, after all...)

Japanese: でも、真宵ちゃんは‥‥
English: But Maya is...

Japanese: くわしい事情はあとで聞くッス。
English: You can ask more about her later, pal.

Japanese: 今は、この部屋に残された 証拠を集めるのが先ッス。
English: Right now, I've got a job to do and that's collecting evidence.

Japanese: （さすが、こんなときは “プロのカオ”になるなあ）
English: (Detective Gumshoe looks like a real professional.)

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと見た感じは 変わらないけど‥‥）
English: (And yet, something about his expression still looks the same...)

Japanese: あの‥‥霧崎先生は、 やっぱりピストルで？
English: So... Dr. Grey was shot with a gun, huh?

Japanese: コメカミを 撃ち抜かれているッス。
English: He was shot in the forehead.

Japanese: ただ‥‥。
English: But...

Japanese: ‥‥ただ？
English: But...?

Japanese: ムネをナイフで 刺されているッス。
English: But he was also stabbed in the chest with a knife.
(...A knife?)

Japanese: 被害者は、刺されたあと ピストルでトドメを刺されたッス。
English: After being stabbed, the final blow was a shot from a gun.

Japanese: あー、今いそがしいッス。 あとでゆっくり見てあげるッス。
English: Ah, I'm really busy right now. I can take a look at it later, alright, pal?

Japanese: （‥‥軽く あしらわれてしまった‥‥）
English: (He didn't even take a glance at it...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・対面の間
English: June 20 Fey Manor Channeling Chamber

Japanese: ‥‥今日はイトノコ刑事、 いないみたいだな‥‥。
English: It looks like Detective Gumshoe isn't here today...

Japanese: 今なら、この部屋を 調べられそうだ。
English: I should take this chance and thoroughly check this room.

Japanese: テッテー的に調べて、 何か手がかりを‥‥
English: If I'm lucky, I might uncover a clue or two...

Japanese: ああら、成歩堂さん。
English: Oh, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あ。こんにちは。
English: Good morning.

Japanese: これから、真宵さまに ご面会に行ってこようかと。
English: I am on my way to go meet Mystic Maya,

Japanese: ちょっと、お差し入れを いたそうかと思いまして。
English: and I thought to bring her some items to make her feel more at home.

Japanese: コトバを失うほどニガいお茶と、 口がひしゃげるほどアマい大福を。
English: Namely, tea so bitter you lose your tongue and jaw-droppingly large strawberry desserts.

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんも よろこぶと思いますよ。
English: I'm sure Maya will be very grateful...

Japanese: “真宵さま”と お呼びなさいませ。
English: That's "Mystic Maya", good sir!

Japanese: （うう‥‥。やっぱり、コワい）
English: (Urk. She is one scary lady.)

Japanese: ‥‥おキノドクな真宵さま。
English: Oh, dear, sweet Mystic Maya...

Japanese: 春美も、ゆうべはずっと 泣いておりました。
English: Pearl wept constantly for you last night, as did I.

Japanese: （‥‥現場を調べるのも ダイジだけど‥‥）
English: (I know it's important for me to search the site,)

Japanese: （おばさんの話も 聞かなきゃな‥‥）
English: (but I should ask her some questions too, while I have the chance...)

Japanese: ‥‥では、そろそろ アタクシは出かけますので。
English: Well, then, I do believe it is time for me to take my leave.

Japanese: はい。 真宵ちゃんによろしく。
English: Alright. Please tell Maya I said hi.

Japanese: じろっ！
English: What was that!?

Japanese: 真宵‥‥サマに。
English: Uh, I mean, Mystic Maya.

Japanese: よろしゅうござあます。
English: I will tell her.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
“修験者の間”に通じる扉。 ずいぶん重そうだ。
English: The Meditation Room is on the other side of this very heavy-looking door.

Japanese: 鉄の錠前がコワれている。 ぼくがやったんだけど‥‥。
English: The lock on it is broken, because, well... I broke it.

Japanese: ムチュウだったとはいえ、 よくコワせたな。
English: It doesn't seem possible, but I managed to break it pretty badly.

Japanese: 人間、やる気になれば なんでもできるんだね。
English: When we want to do something bad enough, people can do the most amazing things.

Japanese: リッパなビョウブだ。 ずいぶん古そうに見える。
English: It's a beautiful and expensive folding screen. It also looks quite old.

Japanese: ‥‥お経‥‥のようなものが 書かれているな。読めないけど。
English: There seems to be some sort of sacred text written on it, but I can't read it.

Japanese: ！　これは‥‥
English: Th-This is...

Japanese: 穴があいてる！　ピストルの 弾丸であいたものかな‥‥？
English: It's a hole! Could this have been made by a bullet?

Japanese: 証拠品《ビョウブ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Folding Screen added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ずいぶん古いビョウブだ。 ふちがボロボロになっている。
English: This folding screen is really showing its age. All of its edges are a bit tattered.

Japanese: くずした字体で、呪文のような ものが書かれている。
English: The lettering is fading too. I think I recognize a few of the characters on here...

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ。 ゼンゼン読めないや。
English: I'm pretty sure that's what it says... Maybe I'll ask Maya about it later...

Japanese: ずいぶん古いビョウブだ。 ふちがボロボロになっている。
English: This folding screen is really showing its age. All of its edges are a bit tattered.

Japanese: くずした字体で、呪文のような ものが書かれている。
English: The lettering is fading too. I think I recognize a few of the characters on here...

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ。 ゼンゼン読めないや。
English: I'm pretty sure that's what it says... Maybe I'll ask Maya about it later...

Japanese: ビョウブのうしろは 床の間になっているようだ。
English: There is some empty space behind the folding screen.

Japanese: ローソクのあかりも とどかず、まっくらだな。
English: The light of the candles can't reach back there, so it's completely dark.

Japanese: ‥‥当然、 だれもかくれていない。
English: I hope no one is hiding back there.

Japanese: 祭壇‥‥というのだろうか。 重いフンイキがただよっている。
English: I guess this is an alter. Whatever it is, it feels very important.

Japanese: 表面がうすくけむった カガミのようなものや、
English: A mirror-like object with a cloudy reflective surface,

Japanese: 青あおとしたサカキが まつられている。
English: and some branches of a sacred tree have been set in a pre-determined arrangement.

Japanese: 霊媒につかうのかな ‥‥やっぱり。
English: I'd guess they're used during the channeling...
