Japanese: きのうのことを お聞きしたいんですけど。
English: I would like to ask you a few questions about what happened yesterday.

Japanese: 事件のことでござあますか。
English: Do you mean that awful tragedy?

Japanese: あの男は‥‥私を殺した‥‥
English: That man... ...murdered me...

Japanese: ‥‥だから‥‥殺してやった‥‥
English: ...so I killed... him...

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵‥‥ちゃん？
English: M-Maya?

Japanese: そこをどくのですッ！
English: Step away from there!

Japanese: この場はアタクシに おまかせなさい。
English: Please leave this area to me.

Japanese: ああたたちは、警察に電話を！
English: Go quickly and inform the police!

Japanese: ‥‥あのあと、この部屋では 何があったんですか？
English: What happened here in this room after I left?

Japanese: そうでござあますわね。
English: Well...

Japanese: ‥‥アタクシはまず、 真宵さまに当て身を。
English: First, I struck Mystic Maya on the head.

Japanese: キゼツさせたわけですか。
English: To make her go unconscious, correct?

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: そして、除霊の術を ほどこしたのでござあます。
English: Next, I performed the Spirit Severing Technique.

Japanese: “除霊の術”‥‥？
English: "Spirit Severing Technique"?

Japanese: 真宵さまの身体には、 看護師の霊が入っておりました。
English: The spirit of that nurse was inside of Mystic Maya's body.

Japanese: それを、冥界へ 帰したわけでござあます。
English: I used the technique to send the nurse's spirit back to the other world.

Japanese: （‥‥明日の法廷、 こんな証言が飛びかうのかな）
English: (I wonder if this kind of testimony is even admissible in a court of law...)

Japanese: ところで、 この部屋はいったい‥‥？
English: By the way, what is this room exactly...?

Japanese: “対面の間”‥‥ ま、アレでござあますわね。
English: The Channeling Chamber?

Japanese: このようなコトが起こらないよう 作られたんでござあますがねえ。
English: This room was created for the sole purpose of preventing such a tragedy as yesterday.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと、ですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 経験のあさい霊媒師が、思念の強い 霊を呼び出してしまうと、
English: Sometimes, when an inexperienced medium calls an especially strong spirit,

Japanese: きのうのように、暴走して しまうことがあるのでござあます。
English: that spirit may become violent and revolt, as you witnessed yesterday.

Japanese: ぼうそう‥‥
English: Revolt? But how?

Japanese: 霊が入った霊媒師の意識は とぎれてしまいます。
English: When a spirit enters a medium's body, she loses her will and her self steps aside.

Japanese: 言ってみれば、霊にカラダを 貸すわけでござあますからねえ。
English: To put it another way, the spirit is borrowing the medium's body, good sir.

Japanese: ‥‥それって、すごく キケンなのでは‥‥？
English: But isn't that extremely dangerous?

Japanese: 経験をつんだ霊媒師ならば、 霊の行動をしばりつけられます。
English: An experienced medium has little problem controlling a spirit.

Japanese: 真宵‥‥サマは、きのうが 初めての霊媒だった‥‥。
English: But Maya... I mean, Mystic Maya... That was her first channeling...

Japanese: そういうわけですから、 カギをかけたのでござあますが。
English: And that was why I insisted on locking the door.

Japanese: まさか、依頼人の身に キケンがおよぶとは‥‥。
English: However, I never thought that would be calling danger upon the client himself...

Japanese: “倉院の呪詞”がブジだったのが、 ただ１つの救いでござあますね。
English: I am grateful that one of our heirlooms was not damaged in this horrible incident.

Japanese: なんですか‥‥それ？
English: And that is...?

Japanese: そこのビョウブですわね。
English: It is that folding screen there. It is the Kurain Sacred Writings.

Japanese: “倉院のツボ”とならんで、 この里の秘宝なのでござあます。
English: Along with the Kurain Sacred Urn, they are this village's most treasured possessions.

Japanese: ‥‥これにもしものことが あったらと思うと‥‥
English: If I ever found out that something had happened to either one...

Japanese: ああ、オソロシイ‥‥。
English: Oh, the inhumanity...

Japanese: （こんなときにビョウブの シンパイとはねえ‥‥）
English: (Odd for her to be worrying about the folding screen at a time like this...)

Japanese: （ただの、ボロボロの ビョウブじゃないか）
English: (I guess this isn't just some dilapidated old screen after all...)

Japanese: あの。 春美ちゃんは、今日は‥‥？
English: So... How is Pearl today...?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: 春美は、アタクシの宝物で ござあます。
English: In this world, Pearl is my most treasured possession.

Japanese: 真宵さまさえいなければ、 家元にもなれる霊力があります。
English: When compared to even Mystic Maya, Pearl has the spiritual power to become a Master.

Japanese: そ、それはスゴいですね。
English: Th-That's very impressive.

Japanese: 綾里の分家の女はこれまで、肩身の せまい思いをしてまいりました。
English: Until now, the women of the branch family have thought themselves to be inferior.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、春美はちがう！
English: But. Pearl is different!

Japanese: あの子の霊力の強さなら、 本家にだって負けるはずがない！
English: Her spiritual strength is so great; even greater than some of the main family!

Japanese: あの子はそう、分家のホコリ！ テンサイなのでござあます！
English: Yes, Pearl is a channeling prodigy and is the pride of the branch family!

Japanese: （うわあ‥‥ 盛り上がっちゃってるぞ‥‥）
English: (Wow... She's really fired up now...)

Japanese: ‥‥というワケで。
English: In any case...

Japanese: ああたとは住む世界がちがいます。 ‥‥おあきらめなさい。
English: Our world is so different from yours, perhaps it is best for you to quit, good sir.

Japanese: “あきらめる”‥‥？
English: "Quit"? Quit what?

Japanese: アタクシ、これから 真宵さまの面会に出かけますのよ。
English: I will be leaving shortly to meet with Mystic Maya.

Japanese: そんなキタナイおもちゃ、 見てるヒマ、ござあません。
English: I simply do not have the time to look at such trivial matters.

Japanese: ‥‥あら！
English: ...Oh my!

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: それは‥‥真宵さまの勾玉。
English: That... That is Mystic Maya's Magatama.

Japanese: そんなものを、どうして ああたがッ！
English: Why would you have this in your possession!?

Japanese: ご、ごめんなさいッ！
English: I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: すぐ、真宵さまに お返しなさいッ！
English: Return it to Mystic Maya at once!

Japanese: ごご、ごめんなさいッ！
English: Y-Yes, madam!

Japanese: （‥‥なぜか、母親に しかられている気分‥‥）
English: (Why did I feel like I was being scolded by my own mother just now...?)

Japanese: ‥‥おや。ああたでも こういうものに興味が？
English: Ah, so even someone such as yourself can take interest in something like this.

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の呪詞、 全六葉が書かれております。
English: This is the Kurain Channeling Technique Sacred Writings. There are 6 panels in all.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: そういうことじゃなくて、 この“穴”なんですけど‥‥。
English: But that's not what caught my eye. You see this hole here...?

Japanese: ま。古いビョウブで ござあますからねえ。
English: It is an old screen, you see.

Japanese: ムシに食われてしまったので ござあましょ。
English: It is only logical that perhaps a bug or worm of some sort is the culprit.

Japanese: （‥‥そうは 見えないんだけどなあ‥‥）
English: (Even though this looks nothing like the eating patterns of any bug I know...)

Japanese: 真宵さまもアレですわねえ。
English: I can hardly believe Mystic Maya has become like this.

Japanese: ああたのような殿方に 引っかかったばかりに、
English: Directly influenced by the likes of you,

Japanese: このような事件に 巻きこまれてしまって‥‥。
English: and becoming caught up in a situation such as this...

Japanese: すみません‥‥。 （‥‥て、ぼくのせいかよ！）
English: I'm sorry... (...Hey, hold on! How is all this MY fault!?)

Japanese: ウチの春美ちゃんにも、 よく言い聞かせておかないと。
English: I told her so many times, as I have also told Pearl,

Japanese: “知らないおじさんに ついて行っちゃダメ”ってねえ。
English: "Do not associate with strange old men you don't know".

Japanese: （言っとくけど、ぼくはまだ おニイさんだからな！）
English: (Well, that shouldn't apply to me then, especially since I'm hardly an "old man" yet!!)

Japanese: その殿方もアレで ござあますわねえ。
English: That doctor was one of those people.

Japanese: 霊にあやまらせるつもりが、 あやめられてしまうとは。
English: Those who intend harm upon the spirits receive harm in return; it's karma at work.

Japanese: やはり、その看護師のイノチを うばったのかもしれませんねえ。
English: It seems that he was the one to take the nurse's life, wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: （‥‥そうなのかな‥‥）
English: (I guess that's possible...)

Japanese: ああたッ！
English: Good sir!

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: Y...Yes?

Japanese: いつのまに、こんな写真を！
English: When did you take this picture!?

Japanese: これ、今ハヤリのアレで ござあましょ！　盗撮‥‥
English: This is one of those... what are they called...? Those... "hidden camera" pictures!

Japanese: ちち、ちがいますよ！
English: N-No!! It's not one of those at all!!

Japanese: ユルしませぬ！ お返しなさいッ！
English: Such an unforgivable offense! You will hand it over to me!

Japanese: だ、ダメです！
English: N-No! I won't!

Japanese: （春美ちゃんの話題には くれぐれも気をつけよう‥‥）
English: (If I ever want to talk about Pearl again, I should be much more careful...)

Japanese: ‥‥いつもカガミで 見かけるカオでござあますわね。
English: It shows the same face as the one I see in the mirror every day.

Japanese: 他に何か？
English: And? Anything else?

Japanese: ござあません。
English: No. That is all.

Japanese: （ぼくはなぜ、こんなものを 見せたんだろう‥‥）
English: (Why in the world did I bother to show this to her!?)

Japanese: この里では、ヨソ者は 歓迎されないのでござあます。
English: We usually do not welcome strangers into our village as they disturb the aura here.

Japanese: 霊気を乱されますのでね。 特にああたのような殿方はねえ。
English: Especially people like you, good sir.

Japanese: なな、なんで特にぼくが‥‥？
English: H-Hey, what do you mean, "especially people like me?"

Japanese: あの真宵さまに、自分のシゴトの 手伝いをさせるなんて‥‥。
English: You selfishly make Mystic Maya help you with your work...

Japanese: イノチがあるだけ ありがたいと思いなさいませ。
English: You should be thankful I have not taken your life for such a disrespectful act.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Y-Yes, madam...

Japanese: この上、ウチの春美に 何かあったら‥‥
English: And even more importantly, remember that if anything were to happen to Pearl...

Japanese: （“何か”ってなんだよ！）
English: (What is that supposed to mean!?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・控えの間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Side Room

Japanese: （ここは‥‥《控えの間》か。 休憩室みたいなものかな）
English: (So this is... the Side Room. Looks like it's more of a break room.)

Japanese: （ふとんがふくらんでる。 だれか寝ているみたいだ）
English: (There is bedding spread out on the floor. It looks like someone's sleeping in one.)

Japanese: （そっとしておくか‥‥）
English: (I shouldn't disturb them...)

Japanese: ふとんがふくらんでいる。 ‥‥だれか寝ているのかな？
English: There's bedding spread out on the floor. Is that someone sleeping in one over there?

Japanese: つかれはてた霊媒師が やすんでるのかもしれない。
English: She's probably an exhausted medium taking a break and catching some z's.

Japanese: ヘタに起こして呪われちゃ たまらないな。
English: If I wake her up, she might be grumpy and throw a spell on me.

Japanese: ‥‥そっとしておこう。
English: It's probably a good idea to not disturb her sleep...

Japanese: そういえば、障子を見るのも ひさしぶりだな。
English: This is the first time I've seen a sliding door like this in person.

Japanese: ここから、わたりろうかと 中庭が見える。
English: From here, I can see the Winding Way and the little garden in the courtyard.

Japanese: 床の間。‥‥ぼくにはどうも、 この空間のイミがわからない。
English: It's a small alcove. I have no idea what it's supposed to mean or symbolize.

Japanese: 事務所にこんなものがあったら、 あっという間に物置だな。
English: If I had one of these in the office, it would end up as storage space.

Japanese: しかしここには、きれいな花と シャレた掛け軸がかざってある。
English: But here, it's decorated with some pretty flowers and a beautiful hanging scroll.

Japanese: これは‥‥クマの置物だな。 台座にプレートがついているぞ。
English: It's a decorative wooden bear sculpture. There's a plate attached to the base.

Japanese: “霊媒の里・倉院” ‥‥なんだ、おみやげ品か。
English: "Kurain Village - The Heart of Channeling" What the!? It's a souvenir.

Japanese: ‥‥ってことは、クマが出るのか？ このへん‥‥。
English: So does this mean it's easy to spot bears here... And for them to spot you...? *gulp*

Japanese: これこれ。 さっきから気になってたんだ。
English: Hmm, now where did this box come from?

Japanese: たしか、きのうはこんなの 置いてなかったよな。
English: I'm sure this wasn't here last night.

Japanese: どうやら、衣装ばこ‥‥ のようだ。ずいぶん大きい。
English: It looks like a box for storing clothes... But it's pretty big for just clothes...

Japanese: ‥‥ほとんどカラだけど、 装束が数枚、たたまれている。
English: It's mostly empty, but a few folded pieces of channeling costumes sit at the bottom.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・控えの間
English: June 19 Fey Manor Side Room

Japanese: （そういえば、さっきここで、 だれか寝ていたけど‥‥）
English: (Hmm, I'm pretty sure there was someone sleeping here earlier...)

Japanese: どなたですかあ‥‥？
English: Like, how can I help you?

Japanese: うわ！ ど、どうも、その。ぼくはあの！
English: Ack! Ah, I'm, uh... That is... I...

Japanese: ‥‥あのお。 そろそろ始まるんですかあ？
English: Um, like, so isn't it about time to start?

Japanese: え。 な‥‥ナニがですか？
English: Huh? Time to start what?

Japanese: 霊媒ですよお、霊媒。
English: You know, like, the channeling!

Japanese: あ、れ、霊媒‥‥？
English: Channeling...?

Japanese: ‥‥いやいやいやいやいや。 それどころじゃないんですよ！
English: Oh, oh, oh, oh!! No, actually, the situation has kind of changed...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 と、言いますと？
English: Huh? Like, what do you mean?

Japanese: オドロかないでください。 ‥‥殺人があったんです！
English: Please stay calm, but... a murder has taken place!

Japanese: サツジン‥‥て、あの。 人が殺される、アレですかあ？
English: Um, so, like... A murder is that thing where, like, one person kills another, right?

Japanese: そうです！ 人が殺される、アレです！
English: Yes! A person has killed another person!

Japanese: まあ‥‥。それは、 なんていうんですか、そのお‥‥
English: Oh, that's... Uh... What are the words I'm looking for...? It's like...

Japanese: ‥‥タイヘンですねえ。
English: ...like totally a bummer.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 （やっと出てきたコトバがそれか）
English: ...Yeah... (After all that effort, that's all she had to say?)

Japanese: あ。申しおくれました。
English: Oh, I'm sorry. I forgot to, like, introduce myself.

Japanese: 私、葉中（はなか）　のどか といいます。
English: My name is Ini Miney.

Japanese: ええと‥‥大学で、 超心理学を研究しています。
English: Um... At the college, I'm researching and studying "parapsychology".

Japanese: ちょうしんりがく？
English: "Parapsychology"?

Japanese: はい。
English: That's right.

Japanese: ええと‥‥ひらたく言えば、その、 オカルト‥‥ですねえ。
English: Um... I think people, like, usually call it, "occult studies" or something.

Japanese: 超常現象とか、超能力とか‥‥。
English: Like supernatural phenomenon, ESP and psychic powers. That sort of stuff.

Japanese: （うわ、アヤシイ‥‥） ぼくは弁護士の成歩堂　龍一です。
English: (That's pretty out there...) I'm Phoenix Wright, attorney at law.

Japanese: まぎらわしいな。 まだふくらんでるぞ、ふとん。
English: You left the blanket a mess. Are you going to fold it?

Japanese: いやあ、すっかり ヒルネしちゃいましたあ。
English: Like I had a really good nap, and, like...

Japanese: 今夜は、思いっきり 夜ふかしできますよお。
English: I was thinking of leaving it like this, so, like, it's all set for tonight.

Japanese: これは‥‥クマの置物だな。 台座にプレートがついているぞ。
English: It's a decorative wooden bear sculpture. There's a plate attached to the base.

Japanese: “霊媒の里・倉院” ‥‥なんだ、おみやげ品か。
English: "Kurain Village - The Heart of Channeling" What the!? It's a souvenir.

Japanese: カワイイでしょお？ 買っちゃいましたあ。
English: Isn't it cuuuuute? I, like, totally bought that!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。 のどかさんのですか。
English: Ah... so it's yours, huh?

Japanese: ええ。この里ってえ、 霊媒とクマが名物ですからあ。
English: Yeah. So, like, this village is really famous for, like, channeling and bears.

Japanese: へえ。 （‥‥って、クマがメイブツ？）
English: Heh. (...Wait. Famous for bears!?)

Japanese: だいじょうぶですよお。
English: But it's, like, OK.

Japanese: クマさんに会ったら、 死んだフリすればいいんです。
English: If you ever, like, see a bear, you just play dead, and they, like, leave you alone.

Japanese: あ、そうなんですか。
English: I-Is that right?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。 抵抗しても、ムダですから。
English: Yeah. Cause, like, fighting back is totally of no use.

Japanese: （なんだそりゃ！）
English: (Has she actually tried to fight one!?)

Japanese: 今日は、どうしてここに‥‥？
English: So what brings you here today?

Japanese: ええと。そのお‥‥ 私が紹介したんですよお。
English: Um, like, so... I was the one who told Dr. Grey about this place.

Japanese: いい霊媒師を知りませんか、 というご相談を受けて‥‥。
English: He asked me, like, "Do you know of a good spirit medium?"

Japanese: （そういえば、霧崎先生も そんなことを言ってたっけ）
English: (Hmm, Dr. Grey did say something about being introduced to this place.)

Japanese: 私、交霊とか多重人格とか、 そのへんが専門ですから‥‥。
English: Cause, like, talking to dead people and, like, multiple personalities are my thing...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。それがどうして、 ここで寝てたんですか？
English: I see... Then why were you taking a nap here?

Japanese: あのお‥‥ダメなんですよねえ、 私って。
English: Like... I wasn't feeling good.
Huh?

Japanese: おソバ。 アレルギーなんですう。
English: I'm, like, allergic to sesame seeds.

Japanese: この里でいただいたお昼の料理に、 ソバ粉が入ってたんですよお。
English: They, like, must've put some in the food they served for lunch.

Japanese: そ、それはそれは。
English: Oh, that doesn't sound fun.

Japanese: ‥‥気分が悪くなって、 ここで寝かせてもらって。
English: So I, like, felt sick, and came in here to, like, sleep.

Japanese: ホント、何しに来たんでしょう、 私ったら‥‥。
English: I totally feel, like, I've wasted my time 'cause I, like, slept a long time.

Japanese: ずっとここで 寝ていたんですか？
English: So you were sleeping here the entire time?

Japanese: そうですねえ。 お昼からずっと‥‥。
English: Yeah. Ever since lunch...

Japanese: じゃあ、事件のコトは、 何も知らない‥‥？
English: So you don't know anything about the murder that took place?

Japanese: ‥‥え？　あ、ああ。 なんにも知りませんよお。
English: Like, what? Oh, that! I, like, totally know nothing.

Japanese: いったい、どなたが 殺されたんですかあ‥‥？
English: So who was, like, you know, killed...?

Japanese: 霊媒を依頼した先生です。 外科医の霧崎先生。
English: The surgeon that requested the channeling, Dr. Grey.

Japanese: お知り合いなんですよね？ 霧崎先生と。
English: You and Dr. Grey knew each other, right?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ 知り合いなんてもんじゃないわ！
English: I don't know any Dr. Grey!

Japanese: あ、そ、そうなんですか？
English: Oh really...?

Japanese: いや。霧崎先生が そう言ってたから‥‥
English: That's not exactly what Dr. Grey said...

Japanese: え！　‥‥あ、ああ。
English: Huh!? ...Well, uh...

Japanese: ‥‥あのお、ムカシ‥‥ そう、患者だったんですよねえ。
English: Like, a long, long time ago... I was, like, a patient, yeah.

Japanese: （この人‥‥。 何か、かくしている‥‥）
English: (She sounds like she's trying to hide something...)

Japanese: ごめんなさあい。 私、おばかさんだからあ、
English: Like, I'm really sorry, but I'm not all that smart...

Japanese: わかりませえん。
English: so I don't, like, get it.

Japanese: （‥‥うーん。 どうもワザとらしいな‥‥）
English: (Hmm. Why does it feel like she's just putting on an act?)

Japanese: 私ってえ、名前おぼえるの ニガテなヒトなんですよお。
English: I'm, like, totally bad at remembering people's names.

Japanese: はあ、そうなんですかあ。
English: Oh?

Japanese: そのヒト‥‥見おぼえは あるんですけどお‥‥。
English: But I, like, remember seeing that face somewhere before, like, I think...

Japanese: そりゃあ ザンネンですねえ‥‥。
English: Well, thanks for trying.

Japanese: （イカン、こっちまで ノンビリした気分になってきた）
English: (I didn't think it was possible for someone to be this much of an airhead...)

Japanese: あ。そのかた。 綾里　真宵さんですよねえ。
English: Oh, hey! That's, like, Maya Fey, right?

Japanese: スゴく有名なヒトなんですよお。 ‥‥そのスジじゃあ。
English: She's, like, totally waaaay famous! Like, well, for people like us, like, you know?

Japanese: （どのスジだ‥‥？）
English: ("Waaaay famous"? Maya?)

Japanese: だってえ、倉院流霊媒道 家元のムスメさんですものねえ。
English: I mean, like, she's, like, the daughter of the Master, right?

Japanese: （うーん‥‥。 ちょっとアマく見てたな、彼女）
English: (Hmm... Well, at least she remembers some things.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・控えの間
English: June 20 Fey Manor Side Room

Japanese: あらあ。あなたは、きのうの。 たしか、歯医者さんの‥‥
English: Oh, hey! Like, you're that guy from yesterday... The, uh, dentist guy...

Japanese: いやいや、弁護士です。
English: No, no, I'm a lawyer.

Japanese: あ、そうでしたねえ。 弁護士の‥‥田中さん。
English: That's right! Um... Mr. Smith, Esquire.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂です。
English: ...That's "Wright". Wait, I mean, you're wrong. My name is "Wright".

Japanese: “タナカ”と“ナルホドー”‥‥。 “な”しか合ってませんねえ。
English: "Smith", "Wright"... Well, I got, like, three letters, at least.

Japanese: （そういう問題じゃない）
English: (I don't think she gets the point...)

Japanese: ところで、どうして あなたがここに？
English: Anyway, why are you here?

Japanese: あ、あのお。 私、大学で超心理学を‥‥
English: So, like, I study this thing called "parapsychology" at this college and...

Japanese: それは、きのう聞きました。 ソバアレルギーのことも。
English: Um, you told me about that yesterday. The sesame allergy, too.

Japanese: それより、なんで今日も まだここにいるんですか？
English: What I'd really like to know is why you're still here today?

Japanese: ああ、そういうことですかあ。 ちゃんと言っていただかないとお。
English: Oh! So, like, that's what you meant! Like, you shoulda been more, like, clear about it!

Japanese: はあ、すみません。
English: S-Sorry. My bad.

Japanese: いいえぇ。 いいんですけどお‥‥。
English: No, it's OK. Like, it's really...

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんでそこで 会話が終わっちゃうんですか！
English: ...That's it? Why'd you stop mid-sentence!?

Japanese: あ。そうでした。 ‥‥で、なんでしたっけえ？
English: Did I? ...So, like... what was I talking about again...?

Japanese: （この人自身、じゅうぶん 超常現象と呼べるぞ‥‥）
English: (If Lotta ever wanted another "genuine mysterious phenomenon", it's right here.)

Japanese: それで？　どうしてあなたが、 今日もここにいるんですか？
English: So? Why are you still here today?

Japanese: ここってえ、すごくベンキョウに なるコトが多いんですよお。
English: Like, there's so much for me to, like, study here!

Japanese: ベンキョウ？
English: Like what?

Japanese: 霊媒師さんたちの修行とかあ、 霊力の遺伝とか‥‥。
English: Like, the medium's training, or like, how they inherit their spiritual power.

Japanese: （たしかに。ホンモノだからな、 この里は‥‥）
English: (I suppose... This village is the real thing, after all.)

Japanese: だから、もう少し置いて もらうことにしたんですう。
English: So, like, I asked if I could stay here for, like, a little longer.

Japanese: （今さらだけど、 ノンキな人だなあ‥‥）
English: (She sure seems like the care-free type, at least, on the surface...)

Japanese: 事件のこと、聞きましたか？
English: Have you heard about the murder?

Japanese: ホント、コワいですよねえ。
English: It's, like, totally scary.

Japanese: （にこにこしながら 言われてもなあ‥‥）
English: (...she says, with a silly smile plastered on her face...)

Japanese: ‥‥何か、心当たりは ありませんか？
English: ...So do you know anything about it that might help?

Japanese: （どうも、つかみどころが ないんだよなあ‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I don't think she has a grip on reality, let alone what's going on here.)

Japanese: 被害者の 霧崎先生のことですけど。
English: About the victim, Dr. Grey...

Japanese: よく知りませえん。
English: I don't know who you're talking about.

Japanese: ‥‥あれえ。たしかそれ、 きのうも聞きましたよねえ。
English: ...Like... Wait, I think you asked about him, like, yesterday, too...

Japanese: （‥‥そう。この質問をしたときの この子の反応‥‥）
English: (That's right... Her reaction yesterday when I asked was...)

Japanese: お知り合いなんですよね？ 霧崎先生と。
English: You and Dr. Grey knew each other, right?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ 知り合いなんてもんじゃないわ！
English: I don't know any Dr. Grey!

Japanese: あ、そ、そうなんですか？
English: Oh really...?

Japanese: いや。霧崎先生が そう言ってたから‥‥
English: That's not exactly what Dr. Grey said...

Japanese: え！　‥‥あ、ああ。
English: Huh!? ...Well, uh...

Japanese: ‥‥あのお、ムカシ‥‥ そう、患者だったんですよねえ。
English: Like, a long, long time ago... I was, like, a patient, yeah.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、何かを かくしてるはずなんだ‥‥）
English: (OK, she is definitely hiding something from me...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかしましたかあ？
English: ...Like, is something wrong?

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥。
English: Would you take a look at this?

Japanese: あ。そういえば、国会議員の みなさんて、そういうバッジを‥‥
English: Hey! It's, like, one of those little badges people in Congress all wear...

Japanese: いやいやいや。 ‥‥だから、弁護士ですよ！
English: No, no, no. I'm a lawyer. L-A-W-Y-E-R!!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？ そうでしたっけ。
English: Like, so is that what you are?

Japanese: わあ！　ステキな勾玉ですねえ。
English: Wow! It's a Magatama!

Japanese: わざわざ、私のために‥‥？
English: Aww, you shouldn't have!

Japanese: ちがいますよ！
English: It's so totally not for you!

Japanese: なんだあ。 おどかさないでくださいよお。
English: Hey! Like, it's not nice to freak me out like that!

Japanese: （どっちのセリフだよ‥‥）
English: (That should be my line...)

Japanese: ごめんなさあい。
English: Like, I'm really sorry, but...

Japanese: せっかくですけどお‥‥ いりません、そんなの。
English: And I know, like, you totally had your heart set on it, but I can't, like, take it.

Japanese: （ダレがあげるって 言ったよ！）
English: (Who said anything about giving this to you!?)

Japanese: この子ですけど‥‥。
English: I wanted to ask you about this girl...

Japanese: ああ、真宵さんですねえ。 家元のムスメさん。
English: Oh, that's, like, Maya Fey. She's, like, the Master's daughter.

Japanese: え。知ってるんですか？
English: You surprise me. You knew that?

Japanese: このギョーカイじゃあ 知らないヒトはいませんよお。
English: Well, like, I totally know everyone related to the occult.

Japanese: なんと言っても、 霊界のアイドルですからあ。
English: Maya is, like, an idol among spirit mediums.

Japanese: （‥‥イヤなアイドルだな‥‥）
English: (Maya an idol? I guess stranger things have happened.)

Japanese: ああ、綾里　キミ子さん。 おセワになってますう。
English: That's Morgan Fey. Like, she's been totally helpful.

Japanese: （‥‥って、写真に向かって おじぎされてもなあ‥‥）
English: (Did she just bow to the picture...?)

Japanese: で、このヒトが何か？
English: So what about her?

Japanese: いや、それを聞きたいんですけど。
English: Oh, nothing in particular. I just wanted to know what you knew about her.

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥。 いい人ですよ。
English: Um... Well, she's, like, a really good person.

Japanese: そうですか。
English: I see.

Japanese: とっても。
English: Like totally.

Japanese: はあ。 （‥‥もう、いいや）
English: OK. (This is going nowhere...)

Japanese: ‥‥このカオ‥‥ どこかで見たような‥‥。
English: Um... This face... Like, I think I've seen it somewhere before...

Japanese: え！ ‥‥じょ、冗談ですよね？
English: What!? ...Y-You're joking, right?

Japanese: やだなあ。 決まってるじゃないですかあ。
English: Ha ha, like, of course I am, silly!

Japanese: （よ‥‥よかった）
English: (Oh, thank goodness...)

Japanese: ‥‥この人のことなんですけど。
English: What do you know about this person...?

Japanese: すみませえん。私、そろそろ おヒルネの時間だから‥‥。
English: I'm, like, sorry, but it's totally, like, time for my nap...

Japanese: ふわあ‥‥ （ぼくまでアクビしてどうする！）
English: *yawn* (Ack! She's even got ME yawning!)

Japanese: キョーミありませえん。 そんなヒト‥‥。
English: And I, like, totally don't care about that person anyway...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・控えの間
English: June 20 Fey Manor Side Room

Japanese: どうも。また来ましたよ。
English: Hello again. We seem to be seeing a lot of each other today.

Japanese: あらあ。かわいい女の子ですねえ。 あの、ムスメさんですかあ？
English: Aww, she's, like, tooootally cute! Is she, like, your daughter?

Japanese: ちち、ちがいますよ！ （ぼくはいくつに見えるんだ！）
English: W-What!? NO! (How old do you think I am!?)

Japanese: 被害者の 霧崎先生のことですけど。
English: I want to ask you about the victim, Dr. Grey.

Japanese: よく知りませえん。
English: I, like, totally don't know him.

Japanese: う、うわ。‥‥なんだこりゃ！
English: Aaack! What is that!?

Japanese: なるほどくん。見えますか？
English: You can see it, can't you, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: あのひとの心を 閉ざしている“錠”です。
English: You can see the "lock" on that person's heart.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって‥‥。
English: Huh, what...?

Japanese: これが、勾玉の霊力。
English: This is the power of the Magatama.

Japanese: なるほどくんにだけ見える 心理錠（サイコ・ロック）です。
English: Only you can see these "Psyche-Locks", Mr. Nick.

Japanese: （‥‥サイコ・ロック‥‥）
English: (Psyche-Locks, huh...)

Japanese: かたく閉ざされている秘密ほど、 ロックの数は多くなりますが‥‥
English: The more someone wants to hide their secret, the more locks you will see.

Japanese: ‥‥このていどならば、 すぐに解除できるでしょう。
English: If it's only one, I think you can easily unlock it.
"Unlock"...? But how?

Japanese: 真宵さまの勾玉を この人につきつけてください。
English: Please use Mystic Maya's Magatama on this person,

Japanese: ロックを解除してみましょう。
English: and let's remove the lock.

Japanese: （よくわからないけど ‥‥やってみるか）
English: (I have no idea what she's talking about, but... Guess I'll give it a try.)

Japanese: ただし。解除に失敗すると、 なるほどくんが痛手を負います。
English: You must be careful, though, Mr. Nick. If you make a mistake, it will hurt you.

Japanese: もし証拠が足りないと思ったら、 途中でやめる勇気も重要です。
English: If you don't think you have the proof you need, you must have the courage to stop.

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥ やってみなきゃ始まらないな）
English: (Well, I've got to start somewhere. Let's give this Psyche-Lock thing a try.)

Japanese: 霧崎先生のこと、 本当に知らないんですか？
English: I'm going to ask you again. Did you know Dr. Grey?

Japanese: 知りませんってばあ。 意外としつこいんですねえ。
English: I totally don't know that guy. Like, you're totally more persistent than a roach!

Japanese: （やっぱり彼女は、 何かをかくしている！）
English: (This girl is hiding something! I'm sure of it!)

Japanese: （‥‥このロックを 解除するしかない、か）
English: (I guess I have no choice but to remove this lock.)

Japanese: （たしか、勾玉を つきつけるんだったな‥‥）
English: (Alright, so first, I have to use Maya's Magatama...)

Japanese: 聞かせてください。 ‥‥霧崎先生のこと。
English: Please, tell me all you know about Dr. Grey.

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥葉中　未実 （みみ）っていうんですけど‥‥。
English: My... My sister's name was Mimi Miney.

Japanese: 霧崎先生の病院で、 看護師をしてたんです。
English: She was, like, a nurse at Dr. Grey's clinic.

Japanese: （やっぱり、そうだったのか‥‥）
English: (That's what I thought...)

Japanese: 霧崎先生‥‥人づかいが アラかったみたいです。
English: I heard that, like, Dr. Grey was really tough on people. Like, a total slave driver.

Japanese: お姉さん、いつも過労で つかれきっていました。
English: My sis was, like, always coming home totally wiped out 'cause she was overworked.

Japanese: ‥‥過労？ それで‥‥交通事故を？
English: Wiped out? So is that why she fell asleep at the wheel?

Japanese: ‥‥お姉さんをそこまで 追いこんだのは、あの人です。
English: That guy, like, drove my sis so hard, so, like, that's why the accident happened.

Japanese: ‥‥医療ミスで１４人の患者を 死なせてしまった、というのは？
English: The one where 14 patients died from malpractice?

Japanese: それも、霧崎先生だと思います。
English: Yeah, like, I think that was the doctor's fault, too.

Japanese: お姉さん、みんなセキニンを 押しつけられて‥‥
English: My sis was pushed by, like, everyone's expectations and, like, her duties and stuff.

Japanese: ‥‥死んじゃったんです。
English: And that pushed her to her death.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: Oh... I'm sorry...

Japanese: もう、いいですかあ？
English: So, like, are we done?

Japanese: ‥‥私の知ってることは みんな話しましたから‥‥。
English: I've, like, already told my story to, like, everyone...

Japanese: いかがですか？　なるほどくん。 ‥‥これが、サイコ・ロックです。
English: Do you understand now, Mr. Nick? This is how a Psyche-Lock works.

Japanese: ‥‥必要がないかぎり、 人の心はのぞくべきじゃないね。
English: ...Well, I don't plan to pry into people's hearts unless I absolutely need to...

Japanese: ‥‥今回の錠は カンタンにはずせましたが、
English: The lock this time was pretty easy, but...

Japanese: これから、もっと手ゴワい ものに出会うこともあるでしょう。
English: you might meet people who aren't as willing to give in later on.

Japanese: 証拠が足りないと思ったら、 くれぐれも深追いしないことです。
English: If you don't have enough proof, be careful and know when to stop trying.

Japanese: まあ、真宵さんのイトコさん‥‥。
English: Um, so you're, like, Mystic Maya's cousin...

Japanese: はい。ふつつか者ですけれど、 霊媒師をめざしております。
English: Yes. I still have a lot to learn, but I'm training to become a medium.

Japanese: へええ。霊界のアイドルめざして がんばってくださいねえ。
English: Aww. Like, keep on trying, cutie! You can, like, be an idol of the medium world, too!

Japanese: （‥‥その表現、 やめてほしいなあ‥‥）
English: (Will you quit calling her an idol! She didn't win a TV show or something!)

Japanese: （サイコ・ロックに挑戦するか？）
English: (Should I try to break the Psyche-Lock?)

Japanese: （サイコ・ロック解除を あきらめた‥‥）
English: (I shouldn't try right now...)

Japanese: ～被害者のこと～
English: -- The Victim --

Japanese: のどかさん。あなたは 霧崎先生を知ってるはずです！
English: Ini Miney. I believe you do know who Dr. Grey is!

Japanese: もお。知らないって言ってる じゃないですかあ。
English: I told you! Like, how many times do I, like, have to repeat myself?

Japanese: そこまで言うんなら、 証拠を見せてくださいよお。
English: If you're, like, so totally sure, then where's, like, your proof?

Japanese: たしかに、あなたと霧崎先生には、 直接の接点はないかもしれません。
English: While it may be possible that you never actually met Dr. Grey in person,

Japanese: ‥‥でも、間接的な接点が あったかもしれない。
English: I do believe you might have at least, indirectly known of him.

Japanese: “かんせつてき”‥‥って、 どういうコトですかあ？
English: Like, what do you mean, like, "indirectly"...?

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、 可能性を示してみよう）
English: (I should try to show that the possibility existed, for now...)

Japanese: （葉中　のどかと霧崎先生に、 何か接点があった可能性‥‥）
English: (Hmm, so, something that shows a possible connection between Ini and Dr. Grey...)

Japanese: これが、その証拠です！
English: Here is your proof!

Japanese: なんですかあ？　それ‥‥。
English: Like, what is that...?

Japanese: うう‥‥ これは関係なかったか‥‥
English: Ouch... I guess it doesn't have anything to do with it, huh?

Japanese: （春美ちゃんが言ってたな‥‥）
English: (Pearls did warn me...)

Japanese: （『シッパイすると、ぼくが 痛手を負う』‥‥って）
English: ("If you make a mistake, it will hurt you," she said.)

Japanese: （証拠が足りないなら、“やめる” という選択も必要かもしれない）
English: (If I don't have enough evidence, I should probably "stop".)

Japanese: （とにかく、法廷記録を見て もう一度、考えてみよう‥‥！）
English: (I should take another look at the Court Record and think it through one more time.)

Japanese: この新聞記事を見てください。
English: Take a look at this newspaper clipping.

Japanese: な、なんですかあ？ ‥‥交通事故？
English: Like, what is it? A story about an accident?

Japanese: 被害者の名前、 読んでみてください。
English: Please read the victim's name.
Um..."Mimi Miney"...

Japanese: “葉中”‥‥めずらしい 名字ですよね。
English: "Miney" is a pretty rare last name, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: この被害者は、 あなたの身内の方なのでは‥‥？
English: Ms. Mimi Miney wouldn't happen to be a relative of yours, would she?

Japanese: よく気がつきましたねえ。 ‥‥私の、お姉さんです。
English: So you noticed. Sh-She was my older sister.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: I'm sorry about your loss...

Japanese: お姉さん、看護師だったんですね？ ‥‥霧崎外科医院の。
English: Your sister... Was she a nurse... Perhaps at Dr. Grey's surgical clinic?

Japanese: 霧崎外科医院‥‥。
English: Grey Surgical Clinic...

Japanese: 言うまでもなく‥‥ 被害者・霧崎　哲郎の病院です！
English: I don't think I need to tell you that the clinic was run by the victim, Dr. Turner Grey.

Japanese: あなたは、お姉さんをとおして 霧崎先生を知っていたのです！
English: Which is how you knew of Dr. Grey; you knew him through your sister!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・わたりろうか
English: June 19 Fey Manor Winding Way

Japanese: （へええ‥‥きれいな庭だな。 ちゃんと手入れされているし）
English: (Wow. This garden is really breath-taking. Someone put a lot of love into it.)

Japanese: （あそこにあるのは‥‥ 焼却炉、かな？）
English: (What is that over there...? ...Is that an incinerator?)

Japanese: （ちょっと、フンイキに 合わないな‥‥）
English: (It looks a little out of place in a garden like this...)

Japanese: おーい！　そこの人！ ちょっとええかー。
English: Hey! You! Hold on, now! This gal's got a few questions to ask!

Japanese: （‥‥む。この大阪弁、どこかで 聞いたことがあるような‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I'm sure I've heard that southern accent somewhere before...)

Japanese: あ、あれッ！ アンタ、ナルホドーやんか！
English: Oh, well I'll be! It's Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ひっさしぶりやなあ！ なんや、１年ぶりぐらいか？
English: How ya been!? Haven't seen you in, what, a year!?

Japanese: え、ええと、たしかあなたは‥‥。
English: Um... You... are... uh...

Japanese: ジャネットさん、でしたっけ？
English: Um, Lotta Hair, was it?

Japanese: ダ、ダレが “じゃねっとさん”やッ！
English: L-L-Lotta Who!? Wait, you best not be making fun of my hair, now!

Japanese: いや、その‥‥なんか、 そんなフンイキだったんで‥‥。
English: Ah, no, um, I wasn't "making fun"... I was "complimenting" you on your fine hair...

Japanese: あーあ。ハクジョーな男やなー。
English: Hmph, I dare say yer one heartless man, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ウチの証言があったからこそ、 アンタの友だちは無罪になった。
English: It was cause of my testimony that yer friend got outta being guilty.

Japanese: ‥‥その恩人を忘れるとはなー。
English: But looks like you already forgot that.

Japanese: あれ‥‥？
English: Hold up...

Japanese: たしかあなた、はじめは 検察側の証人として、
English: You were working with the prosecution at first as one of their witnesses,

Japanese: やる気マンマンでぼくたちを 追いつめていたような‥‥
English: and came after us as if we were a piece of sirloin steak...

Japanese: そんなん、ササイなコトや。 ホンマ、ケツの穴の小さな男やな。
English: Minor detail. Stop being so uptight; lettin' little things get in the way.

Japanese: ナツミや、ナツミ。 大沢木（おおさわぎ）　ナツミ。
English: The name's Hart. Lotta Hart. Don't you go forgetting it!

Japanese: あ、そう、それ！
English: Oh yeah! That's it!

Japanese: ええかァ。今日こそ 撮ったるでー、スクープ写真。
English: Now listen here. I'm here to take some pictures today. Gonna get myself a real scoop.

Japanese: あ。まだ目ざしてたんですか。 フリーカメラマン。
English: Ah, so I guess you're still at it? Being a photographer, I mean.

Japanese: メッチャはげんどるでー。
English: You betcha! The hardest working one out there, I reckon!

Japanese: “オカルト報道業界にこの人あり” って言わしたるんや！
English: Didn't I say I was gonna make a name for myself as a famous "paranormal photographer"!?

Japanese: たしか、ナツミさん‥‥ですよね。
English: You're Lotta Hart, right...?

Japanese: ビンゴやッ！
English: Bingo!

Japanese: “オカルト報道業界にこの人あり” 大沢木（おおさわぎ）　ナツミや！
English: I'm the one and only "paranormal photographer", Lotta Hart!

Japanese: へえ。まだ目ざしてたんですか、 フリーカメラマン‥‥。
English: Ah, so I guess you're still at it? Being a photographer, I mean.

Japanese: そらそうや！
English: Of course!

Japanese: 今日こそ、ホンモノの スクープ写真、モノにしたるんや！
English: And today, I'm gonna get myself a real scoop! You wait and see!

Japanese: すみません。だれでしたっけ？
English: Sorry, but who are you again?

Japanese: ‥‥コレやッ！　都会のオトコは 冷たいゆうけど、
English: Oh Lawdy! I've met some cold men in my days, but you...

Japanese: アンタはその中でも チョラモンマ級や！
English: You're in an icebox of yer own!

Japanese: ちょもらんま‥‥
English: Come on, am I really that bad...?

Japanese: 思い出してみぃ！
English: Put yer noggin to some use! You've gotta remember who I am!

Japanese: ウチの証言があったればこそ、 アンタの友だちが無罪になった！
English: It was cause of my testimony that yer friend got outta being guilty.

Japanese: あっ！　あなた、あのときの‥‥！
English: Ah! You're the one from that trial!

Japanese: せや。思い出したみたいやな。
English: See, I knew you could remember if you tried!

Japanese: たしか、検察側の証人として やる気マンマンで出てきて、
English: You were working with the prosecution at first as one of their witnesses,

Japanese: ぼくたちをガンガン 追いつめた、オニのような‥‥
English: and came after us as if we were a piece of sirloin steak...

Japanese: ま。ま。ええわええわ。 ムカシのハナシや。
English: Why don't we let bygones be bygones, now. It's yesterday's news, right?

Japanese: ウチはナツミ。 大沢木（おおさわぎ）　ナツミや。
English: Name's Lotta Hart.

Japanese: ホンマ、忘れんといてや。
English: Don't you go forgetting it, ya hear!?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 今日はどうして、ここに‥‥？
English: Um... So what brings you here today?

Japanese: ハッ！　聞くまでもないやろ！ スクープやスクープ！
English: Hah! You should already know! I'm here for the big scoop! I can feel it!

Japanese: へえ‥‥まだ目ざしてたんですか？ フリーカメラマン。
English: Ah, so I guess you're still at it? Being a photographer, I mean.

Japanese: メッチャはげんどるでー。
English: You betcha! The hardest working one out there, I reckon!

Japanese: “オカルト報道業界にこの人あり” って言わしたるんや！
English: Didn't I say I was gonna make a name for myself as a famous "paranormal photographer"!?

Japanese: あ、そうそう！
English: Oh, that's right!

Japanese: こんなところで ダベってる場合やないんや。
English: This ain't no time to be wastin' time!

Japanese: そろそろ始まるで！　霊媒。
English: The channeling's about to get started!

Japanese: そうなんですか！
English: Is it that time already!?

Japanese: すぐに《修験者の間》に集まれ、 ってコトや。
English: We all should hurry up and get to the Meditation Room.

Japanese: ‥‥ほな、伝えたで！
English: ...What are you waiting around for!? Come on!

Japanese: （ナツミさん‥‥か）
English: (Lotta Hart, huh?)

Japanese: （この前の事件ではホント、 ヒドい目にあったなあ）
English: (I've been judging her harshly because of that case,)

Japanese: （まあ今となっては、いい思い出と 言えなくもないけど‥‥）
English: (but, I guess looking back, we had some good times, too...)

Japanese: これまたずいぶんと 古いツボだな。
English: It's a really old urn.

Japanese: そこら中に ヒビが入っているぞ。
English: The ashes of a person long dead are inside.

Japanese: 何か、いわれのある ものなんだろう‥‥。
English: It looks like something with a long history...

Japanese: キョーミがないので、 とりあえずほっとくか。
English: Other than that, it's not very interesting, so time to move on.

Japanese: あそこにも部屋がある みたいだ。
English: It looks like there's a room over there, too.

Japanese: 落ちついたフンイキが ただよっている。
English: A peaceful feeling is emanating from it.

Japanese: 小型の焼却炉だ。
English: It's a small incinerator.

Japanese: この里には、ゴミの収集車は まわってこないのだろう。
English: I don't think garbage trucks come all the way out here to a little village like this.

Japanese: ゴミは、それぞれの家で 処理しているようだ。
English: So every family has to burn their own trash.

Japanese: 灯ろうに、ししおどし。 リッパな庭だなあ。
English: A splendid garden with a small lamp and traditional decorations.

Japanese: ‥‥遊ぶには ちょっとせまいけど。
English: It's a bit small to walk around in...

Japanese: でも、あの焼却炉が 気になるなあ。
English: Although, that incinerator piques my curiosity...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・わたりろうか
English: June 19 Fey Manor Winding Way

Japanese: （あいかわらず、 だれもいない、か‥‥）
English: (There's no one here... as usual.)

Japanese: うわっ！ ‥‥や、やあ。また会ったね。
English: Ack! ...H-Hey... How are you?

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、ていうんだよね？
English: You're Pearl, right?

Japanese: （やれやれ、まただんまりか‥‥）
English: (Hmm. Silent as ever...)

Japanese: （‥‥？　ちいさな手に、 何かにぎりしめてるぞ‥‥）
English: (...? What's that she's got in her hand?)

Japanese: （‥‥どこかで見たような‥‥）
English: (I've seen it somewhere before...)

Japanese: あのさ‥‥。
English: Um...

Japanese: （‥‥ナニがいけないんだ！ この髪型か‥‥？）
English: (...What is it!? Is it my hair? Is it too spiky? Not spiky enough!?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・わたりろうか
English: June 20 Fey Manor Winding Way

Japanese: （‥‥今日は、 春美ちゃんは見あたらないな）
English: (Little Pearl isn't here today...)

Japanese: （こんな時間だし、学校かな？）
English: (Maybe she's at school?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・わたりろうか
English: June 20 Fey Manor Winding Way

Japanese: わっ！ び‥‥びっくりした。
English: Ack! Y-You surprised me!

Japanese: どう？　最近。
English: And how are you today?

Japanese: （うーん、さりげない世間話にも 反応してくれないか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I guess she isn't open to friendly chatting...)

Japanese: （‥‥ん？　また、 手に何か持ってるな）
English: (Huh? She's still holding something in her hand...)

Japanese: （‥‥たしか、きのうも 持っていたよな‥‥あれ）
English: (...It looks like the same thing she had yesterday...)

Japanese: あの‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Um, Mr. Nick...?
Yes, Pearls? I can call you that, right? You're too cute to just call you Pearl.

Japanese: その勾玉‥‥よろしいですか？
English: Sure, Mr. Nick... Umm... Are you sure it's OK for me to have this Magatama?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、いいよ。 春美ちゃんにあげる。
English: Yeah, it's OK. I was told to give it to you.

Japanese: いけません、そんな！
English: But, I can't accept something like this!

Japanese: ‥‥わたくしはただ、 この勾玉に霊力を‥‥。
English: ...All I really should do is charge this Magatama with spiritual energy...

Japanese: れいりょく‥‥？
English: Spiritual energy?

Japanese: これをお持ちください。 きっと、役に立ちます。
English: Please take this. I'm sure it will be helpful.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやって？
English: ...What do you mean?

Japanese: 人の心の秘密がわかります。
English: It will let you see people's secrets.

Japanese: ココロの‥‥ヒミツ？
English: See people's... secrets...?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですわ。わたくし、 しばらくお供いたします。
English: ...If it's alright with you, I would like to accompany you for a little while.

Japanese: 秘密をかかえた人に会ったとき、 その勾玉のチカラをお教えします。
English: I can explain the Magatama's power to you when we meet someone hiding a secret.

Japanese: あのさ‥‥。
English: Um...

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: きゃ、“きゃあ”‥‥？
English: H-Huh??

Japanese: （何か、気を引けるようなものが あればいいんだけど‥‥）
English: (If only I had something that would catch her interest. Maybe then she'd talk to me.)

Japanese: ちょっと、見てくれるかな‥‥
English: Can you take a look at this? Please?

Japanese: （うう‥‥ぼくのカオを じっと見つめてるぞ‥‥）
English: (Urk... She's really scrutinizing my face...)

Japanese: （そそ、そんなに 見つめないでくれえ！）
English: (Th-That's enough already! I'm not that shifty-looking, am I!?)

Japanese: そうだ、これ‥‥。 真宵ちゃんが、きみに、って。
English: Oh yeah. Maya said to give this to you...

Japanese: （うわわわわ、しまった！）
English: (Ack! I'm in trouble now!!)

Japanese: （こんなところを見られたら、 キミ子さんに殺される！）
English: (If Morgan sees us like this, I'll be the next one they're channeling...)

Japanese: ‥‥それは‥‥真宵さまの‥‥
English: Th-That's... That's Mystic Maya's...

Japanese: ‥‥え？ （しゃ、しゃべったぞ‥‥）
English: ...Huh!? (Sh-She actually spoke!)

Japanese: あなたは‥‥？
English: Who are you...?

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくは成歩堂　龍一。 真宵ちゃんとは‥‥
English: I-I'm Phoenix Wright. I, uh, worked with Maya...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったのですか‥‥
English: ...Y-You worked with Mystic Maya...?

Japanese: あなたがあの、 なるほどくんなのですね。
English: You... You're Mr. Nick, right?

Japanese: ‥‥え？ （“なるほどくん”って‥‥）
English: ...Uh, excuse me? (I bet I know who she picked "Nick" up from...)

Japanese: もちろん、存じあげております。
English: I know who you are.

Japanese: あの。 真宵さまの‥‥大切なヒト。
English: You're... You're Mystic Maya's... "special someone"...

Japanese: ななな‥‥ナニィィ！
English: WH-WH-WH-WHAT!?

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ そう！　もちろん、そうですわ！
English: So then... Of course!

Japanese: 真宵さまを 助けてくださるのですね！
English: You're going to help Mystic Maya, aren't you?

Japanese: そうなのですねっ！
English: That's what you're going to do, right?

Japanese: まま、まあ。 そのつもりだけど‥‥。
English: W-Well, yeah... I will...

Japanese: ああ‥‥ なんて美しいお話なのでしょう！
English: Oh wow... It's like a beautiful fairy tale!

Japanese: その、ひたむきなヒトミにやどる 光こそ、オトナの愛のアカシ‥‥
English: That earnest look shining brightly in your eyes... It must be true love...

Japanese: ‥‥ぐはあッ！
English: ...Wh-What!?

Japanese: （なんでこんな小さな子の前で 赤面しなくちゃならんのだ！）
English: (Why am I being boiled into a bright red lobster by this little kid!?)

Japanese: わたくし、 真宵さまがうらやましいです。
English: I'm so jealous of Mystic Maya!

Japanese: ああ、なんてステキな‥‥
English: Ah, what a wonderful relationship...

Japanese: たた、たのむからやめて。 恥ずかしくて死ぬ。
English: W-Wait... I... uhh... I mean... We aren't... Things aren't like...

Japanese: ふふ。奥ゆかしいのですね。
English: Hee hee, I can tell you're a good person.

Japanese: わかりました！
English: Alright, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: わたくし、ささやかながら 協力させていただきます！
English: I may be small, but I'm going to help you in any way I can!

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、 真宵ちゃんの友だちなの？
English: Pearl, are you friends with Maya?

Japanese: 友だちなんてそんな、 おそれおおい‥‥。
English: I feel very grateful to be friends with her... She's so great.

Japanese: 真宵さまは、わたくしのアコガレ、 クモの上の人です。
English: I want to be like Mystic Maya when I grow up. I really look up to her.

Japanese: へええ。 真宵ちゃんがねえ‥‥。
English: Wow, I had no idea Maya was so... revered.

Japanese: ふつうなら、真宵さまを“ちゃん” づけで呼ばれるのを聞くだけで、
English: Usually when people don't use Mystic Maya's title,

Japanese: わたくし、 ひっぱたいてしまうところです。
English: I get mad.

Japanese: そ、それはゴメン。
English: O-Oh, sorry.

Japanese: でも、なるほどくんは いいのです。
English: But if it's you, Mr. Nick, then it's OK.

Japanese: だって‥‥真宵さまの ‥‥想われビトですものね。
English: Because... Because... you're "special" to her.

Japanese: （だから、 なんでそうなるんだあ‥‥）
English: (Where in the world did she get an idea like that??)

Japanese: ‥‥ところで‥‥。
English: By the way...

Japanese: シツレイですが、 なるほどくんは、どこの流派に？
English: this may be rude, but which channeling school are you from?

Japanese: りゅうは？
English: Which channeling school?

Japanese: はい。わたくしは 倉院流をたしなんでおりますが。
English: Yes. For example, I study the Kurain technique.

Japanese: いやいやいや。 ぼくは弁護士だよ。弁護士。
English: Ah, I get it! No, no, no. I'm a lawyer. A lawyer.

Japanese: ‥‥べんごし？
English: ...Law...yer?

Japanese: そう。
English: Yup.

Japanese: ‥‥それは霊媒師とは、いかなる 関係にあたるのでしょうか？
English: ...Is that related to spirit mediums in any way...?

Japanese: （‥‥いかなるカンケーにも あたらないと思うけど‥‥）
English: (Um... None that I know of...)

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして、 知らないの？　弁護士。
English: Wait, are you telling me you don't know what a lawyer is?

Japanese: もうしわけありません‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, but I have no idea.

Japanese: （そういえば真宵ちゃん、この子を “世間知らず”とか言ってたな）
English: (Maya did say Pearl doesn't know much about the outside world...)

Japanese: ところで、 その手に持っているのは？
English: So what's that you've got there, Pearl?

Japanese: あ‥‥これ。 きのう、ひろいました。
English: Oh, this? I found it yesterday.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あ。ほしいのでしたら、 さしあげますけど‥‥？
English: If you want it, I can give it to you.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんの宝物を とっちゃ悪いからね。
English: I can't take something this important from you, Pearl.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: （でも、あのカギ。 どこかで見たような‥‥）
English: (But I'm sure I've seen that key somewhere before...)

Japanese: （‥‥重大な手がかりに なるかもしれない‥‥）
English: (...and it might be an important clue later on...)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、せっかくだから、 もらっておこうかな。
English: Alright, well, if you really want to give it to me...

Japanese: ふふ。なるほどくん、 子どもみたいですね。
English: Hee hee. You look like a child at a toy store, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: （‥‥上品に笑いとばされた）
English: (...She not only speaks in a refined manner, but laughs in one, too!)

Japanese: 証拠品《黒いカギ》を 春美ちゃんからもらった。
English: Black Key added to the Court Record.

Japanese: これなんだけど‥‥。
English: So this is...

Japanese: 真宵さまの勾玉ですね。
English: That is Mystic Maya's Magatama.

Japanese: 倉院の里に伝わる、 とても貴重な宝玉です。
English: It's one of the most important heirlooms of Kurain Village.

Japanese: どうか、 大切にしてくださいね。
English: Please make sure to take good care of it.

Japanese: う、うん。 （いいのか、そんなダイジなもの）
English: Y-Yeah, I will. (Is it really OK for me to have such a priceless object?)

Japanese: このカギなんだけど‥‥。
English: About this key...

Japanese: きのう、お庭で遊んで いたときに、ひろいました。
English: I found it while I was playing in the garden yesterday.

Japanese: ぼくも、どこかで 見たことがあるんだよ。
English: I've seen it before too, you know?

Japanese: ‥‥？　そうなのですか？
English: Really?

Japanese: ‥‥あら。なるほどくんも こういうものに興味が？
English: Are you interested in Kurain's traditional things too, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の呪詞が 書かれているのですよ。
English: The Kurain Sacred Writings are on this folding screen.

Japanese: へえ。そうなんだ。
English: Oh? Is that what's written on it?

Japanese: でも、それより この“穴”のことだけど‥‥。
English: Actually, what caught my eye was this "hole" in it...

Japanese: とても古い ビョウブですから。
English: It's a very old screen,

Japanese: ムシさんに食べられて しまったのだと思います。
English: so I think that maybe bugs made it by eating through it.

Japanese: （‥‥そうは 見えないんだけどなあ‥‥）
English: (...It sure doesn't look like something bugs can make...)

Japanese: 何かお役に立てれば いいのですが、
English: I'm sure it is helpful to you in some way,

Japanese: なにぶん今は修行中の身で、 特に思いつくことは‥‥。
English: but I'm still in training, so I don't know anything about it.

Japanese: 本当に、ほんとうに もうしわけございません。
English: I'm really, deeply sorry.

Japanese: い、いえいえ。
English: No, no, no, it's OK.

Japanese: こちらこそ、ツマラナイ ものを見せちゃって‥‥
English: I shouldn't have shown you something so trivial anyway.

Japanese: どうもスミマセン。 （‥‥て、何かオカシイ気がする）
English: I'm very sorry. (This feels so weird, talking to a kid like this...)

Japanese: あ。おかあさま。
English: Oh, it's my mother!

Japanese: わたくしを、とても大切に してくださるのですよ。
English: She takes really good care of me and loves me very much.

Japanese: 《ぶんけのほこりだ》と言って。 ‥‥意味はわかりませんけど。
English: She says I'm the "pride of the branch family"... But I don't know what that means.

Japanese: （《分家》‥‥か。春美ちゃんには まだ、ムズカシイかな）
English: ("Branch family"... That concept is probably still too hard for little Pearl...)

Japanese: 真宵さまは、わたくしの アコガレです。
English: I look up to Mystic Maya a lot. She's my role model.

Japanese: あたまがよくて、うつくしくて、 やさしくて、あたたかくて、
English: She's so smart, and pretty, and kind, and loving.

Japanese: いつもほがらかで、カゼもひかず、 好きキライもせず、早寝早起き、
English: She's always cheerful, never gets sick, isn't picky, and has good sleeping habits.

Japanese: わたくしより、かけっこも早いし、 ごはんを食べるのも早いし、
English: She always wakes up before me, and always eats breakfast before me too.

Japanese: ‥‥それに、こんなステキな 殿方と、オトナの
English: And she has you; such a wonderful person with whom she can share a lo--

Japanese: わー！　よくわかったよ。 （よっぽど好きなんだな‥‥）
English: Ack! OK, OK! I get the idea! (She really thinks the world of Maya, doesn't she?)

Japanese: わたくしも、真宵さまのように 髪を長くしたいのですが、
English: To tell you the truth, I really want to grow my hair out, just like Mystic Maya.

Japanese: おかあさまが ゆるしてくれないのです。
English: But my mother won't let me.

Japanese: でも、その髪型も にあってるよ。
English: Your hair now suits you quite well, though, I think.

Japanese: そそ、そうですか。 ああ、ありがとうございます。
English: R-Really? Th-Thank you!

Japanese: ‥‥もじもじ。
English: *fidget, fidget*

Japanese: ‥‥もじもじ。
English: *fidget, fidget*

Japanese: あまり見かけない おカオですね。
English: Hmm, I'm sorry I don't know who this person is.

Japanese: この里の人じゃ ないからね。
English: I guess that makes sense... You've never left Kurain village, after all.

Japanese: わたくし、この里から 出たことがないものですから。
English: I'm really sorry, Mr. Nick. I just don't really know anyone outside the village.

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: June 20 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （どうすればいいんだ！ もう、時間がないぞ‥‥）
English: (What am I supposed to do!? I'm almost out of time!)

Japanese: （彼女を助けるなんて‥‥ ぼくにできるのか？）
English: (Can I really do anything? Can I really save her...?)

Japanese: ‥‥お待たせしました。
English: Sorry to keep you waiting.

Japanese: （ん‥‥ 真宵ちゃんが来たのかな）
English: (Huh? Must be Maya...)

Japanese: ひさしぶりね。なるほどくん。
English: It's been a long time, hasn't it, Phoenix?
(That voice...)

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: しばらく見ないうちに、 少したくましくなったみたい。
English: Even without me being here, it looks like you've learned to stand on your own.

Japanese: （千尋さん‥‥。真宵ちゃんの お姉さんで、ぼくの師匠だ）
English: (Mia... She's Maya's older sister, and my mentor.)

Japanese: （一流の弁護士だったんだけど、 ある事件で、帰らぬ人となった）
English: (She was a top-notch defense lawyer, but a certain case forced her into "retirement".)

Japanese: （でも‥‥ピンチになると、 こうしてぼくを助けてくれる）
English: (But... whenever I'm in trouble, she comes to help, just like this.)

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんの身体に、 そのタマシイを宿らせて‥‥）
English: (Her spirit comes back from the other world and borrows Maya's body for a bit...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 弁護士がそんなカオしちゃダメ。
English: Phoenix, you can't make that kind of face in front of your client.

Japanese: 弁護士はね。ピンチのときほど ふてぶてしく笑うものよ。
English: A lawyer is someone who smiles no matter how bad it gets, and especially when it's bad.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥。
English: Mia...

Japanese: それが、最後に笑うための条件。
English: You can't smile at the end if you haven't been smiling the whole way there.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、依頼人に 見せるべきカオじゃないわ。
English: In any case, the face you're making now is no face to show a client, Phoenix.

Japanese: で‥‥でも！
English: B-But!

Japanese: さあ、話を聞かせて。
English: So, tell me all about it.

Japanese: また、妹がメイワク かけちゃったみたいね‥‥。
English: I'm going to guess that my sister is in a lot of trouble again...

Japanese: （ぼくは‥‥この２日間の ことを、ゼンブ話した）
English: (I told her everything about what had happened in the last two days.)

Japanese: （千尋さんは、目をつぶって 考えこんでいた‥‥）
English: (Mia closed her eyes, deep in thought, while she listened...)

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことなの‥‥。
English: ...I see...

Japanese: 千尋さん。ぼくは‥‥ どうすればいいんですか？
English: Mia... What am I supposed to do?

Japanese: この場合、弁護士のとるべき 立場はハッキリしてるわね。
English: It's pretty clear what a good lawyer does in this situation.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！ 教えてください！
English: A-And that is!? Tell me, please!

Japanese: 決まってるわ。 断固として主張するの。
English: Believe in your convictions.

Japanese: もちろん‥‥、完全無罪をね。
English: And fight for the complete acquittal of your client.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが‥‥ “完全無罪”なんですか！
English: You think Maya's not guilty!? How can you be so sure!?

Japanese: ‥‥ひとつ、いいことを 教えてあげるわ。
English: I know she is. And I'll give you a hint as to why.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: 霊媒師は、夢を見ない。
English: Mediums can't have dreams.
Can't have "dreams"...?

Japanese: あなたの話では、霊媒中、 真宵は夢を見ていたそうね。
English: From what you told me, it sounds like Maya was having a dream while channeling.

Japanese: ええ。‥‥たしか、死んでしまって 土の中に埋められる夢を‥‥。
English: Yeah... She said she dreamt that she had died and had been buried in the ground.

Japanese: それはあり得ないの。
English: But that is impossible.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたも聞いてるはずよ。 霊媒中の霊媒師は、意識がない。
English: You've heard it from her, I'm sure. When a medium channels, her own spirit disappears.

Japanese: ‥‥夢なんて、 見られるはずがないわ。
English: Which means that it's impossible for her to dream during that time.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ それって‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Then... What does this mean!?

Japanese: 真宵はワナにはめられた。 ‥‥そう考えるのが自然ね。
English: I think it's safe to bet that Maya was set-up.
A set-up!?

Japanese: ハッキリしたことは、 明日の法廷で暴くしかないけど。
English: It's up to you to blow the lid on this case tomorrow and show how she was set-up.

Japanese: でも‥‥無罪にしようにも、 なんの手がかりもなくちゃあ！
English: How am I supposed to prove her innocence when I have nothing to go on!?

Japanese: 手がかりなら、 ちゃんと持ってるはずよ。
English: If you're looking for a clue, it's already in your hands.

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくが‥‥ですか？
English: I-It is!?

Japanese: 文字どおり、あなたは “事件のカギ”をにぎっているわ。
English: Just as it sounds, Phoenix, you already hold the "key".

Japanese: （‥‥そうなのか‥‥？）
English: (R-Really...?)

Japanese: さあ、私に見せて。 あなたの“カギ”を。
English: Come on, show me the "key" to this case.
(The "key", huh?)

Japanese: （よし‥‥見せてみるか。 ぼくの持っている“カギ”を！）
English: (Alright, I'll show it to her; the "key" I hold.)

Japanese: ‥‥もういいわ、なるほどくん。
English: You already know everything you need to know.

Japanese: あなたにも、ちゃんと わかってるみたい。
English: You know what the "key" to this case is...

Japanese: この事件の“カギ”が‥‥。
English: That is enough...

Japanese: で‥‥でも！
English: But... But!

Japanese: カンジンの犯人がわからなきゃ、 勝ち目はありません！
English: How can I win tomorrow without knowing who the real murderer is!?

Japanese: いったい、だれが‥‥？
English: Who... Who could have...?

Japanese: （‥‥そのときぼくは、 この日最後の‥‥）
English: (At the time, I didn't know,)

Japanese: （そして、 最大の驚きを味わった‥‥）
English: (but this day was going to end with a turn for the surprising...)

Japanese: ち、千尋さん！ ‥‥それは‥‥？
English: M-Mia!? Wh-What...!?

Japanese: どうしたの、なるほどくん？
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: （‥‥そうか。サイコ・ロックは ぼくにしか見えないんだ‥‥）
English: (That's right. Only I can see the Psyche-Locks...)

Japanese: （千尋さんは‥‥真犯人のこと ‥‥何か知っているんだ！）
English: (Which means... Mia must know something about the real murderer!)

Japanese: （しかも、ココロにかたく カギをかけたヒミツとして‥‥）
English: (But for this to be something that she would hide under lock and key from even me...)

Japanese: （いったいこれは‥‥ どういうことなんだ！）
English: (What in the world is going on!?)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 千尋さん、これ‥‥。
English: Mia...

Japanese: ふふ‥‥なつかしいわね、 弁護士バッジ‥‥。
English: *chuckle* Wow, this badge brings back a lot of memories, doesn't it...?

Japanese: もう一度、あなたと 立ってみたいわ。弁護席に。
English: I would love to stand with you at the defense table one more time.

Japanese: （千尋さん‥‥）
‥‥これは‥‥ 真宵の勾玉ね。
English: This is... Maya's Magatama, isn't it?

Japanese: どうしてあなたが、これを？
English: Why do you have it...?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが くれたんですけど‥‥
English: Maya gave it to me, but...

Japanese: そうなの‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: これは、私にとっても 大切なものなの。
English: This is something very important to me.

Japanese: ダイジにしてね。
English: Take good care of it, OK?

Japanese: （‥‥とてもやさしい目で 勾玉をながめている‥‥）
English: (She looks at it with such a soft look in her eyes...)

Japanese: これ、見てください。
English: Mia, have you seen this key before?

Japanese: そう、そのカギ‥‥。
English: Ahh, the key...

Japanese: この事件の、すべての謎を 解くためのカギよ。
English: It's literally, the key to understanding everything that's happened in this case.

Japanese: これが‥‥？
English: This key?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 今、あなたがそれを持っている。
English: Phoenix, listen. Right now, that key is sitting in your hand.

Japanese: でも、そんなことは、あり得ない。 ‥‥ムジュンしてるの。
English: However, it shouldn't be. It contradicts the "facts".

Japanese: （‥‥どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (What does she mean...?)

Japanese: その証拠は、まちがいなく 明日の裁判のキメ手になるわ。
English: I'm certain this key will be the piece of evidence that makes your case tomorrow.

Japanese: あの。これなんですけど‥‥
English: Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: ‥‥もっと、手がかりに なりそうなものはない？
English: ...Do you have anything more useful?

Japanese: 時間がないわ。 他のものを見せて。
English: There's no time left. Try to show me something that will help you in tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: ‥‥どこかで見たことが あるわね。
English: I've seen this man somewhere before...

Japanese: ヘンな名前の刑事さん。 ‥‥尋問したことがあるわ。
English: He had a strange name... and I'm almost sure I've questioned him before.

Japanese: たしか、 自信マンマンで出てきて、
English: I remember him making a show of confidence at first,

Japanese: ５分後にはもう、 しどろもどろになっちゃって。
English: but after five minutes, he breaks down and...

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、 カワイソウだったかしら。
English: ...you end up feeling bad for the poor guy...

Japanese: はみちゃん！ ‥‥大きくなったわね‥‥。
English: Pearly! She's really grown up...

Japanese: 私が倉院の里を出てから、 もうずいぶんになるわ。
English: I left Kurain Village a long time ago.

Japanese: 私のことなんて、もう おぼえてないでしょうね‥‥。
English: She probably doesn't remember a thing about me by now...

Japanese: おばさまには メイワクをかけたわ。
English: I'm afraid I might have caused Aunt Morgan a lot of stress.

Japanese: 私が里を出てから、真宵の めんどうを見ていただいて。
English: When I left Kurain, I left Maya in my aunt's care.

Japanese: ‥‥こんな事件が 起こってしまうなんて‥‥。
English: And now something like this happens...

Japanese: 私がこうして真宵の身体に 宿っていると、
English: I can't see Maya...

Japanese: あの子と会うことはできない。
English: when I'm in her body like this...

Japanese: ‥‥でも、感じるわ。 あの子、やっぱりさびしいの。
English: But I can tell. She's very lonely and sad.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥支えてあげてね。
English: Take good care of her, will you, Phoenix?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。
English: I-I will...

Japanese: そう。私はもう、 死んでるのよね。
English: That's right. I'm already dead.

Japanese: 今、あなたとこうして お話しすることができる‥‥。
English: But I feel very grateful to have been born a Fey...

Japanese: 綾里の家に生まれて、 本当によかったわ。
English: so I can talk with you like this...

Japanese: ‥‥ごめんなさい。 そのカオには、見おぼえがないわ。
English: I'm sorry, but I don't remember who that is...
